conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→!!! important
  2→    Up and until Ktlint version `0.47`, experimental were located in a separate `experimental` rule set. As of Ktlint version `0.48`, each rule set can optionally define experimental rules.
  3→
  4→All experimental rules described below are part of the `standard` rule set of Ktlint. To enable all experimental rules (from all rule sets), set `editorconfig` property below:
  5→```ini
  6→[*.{kt,kts}]
  7→ktlint_experimental=enabled
  8→```
  9→Also see [enable/disable specific rules](../configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules).
 10→
 11→## Binary expression wrapping
 12→
 13→Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
 14→
 15→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 16→
 17→    ```kotlin
 18→    fun foo() {
 19→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 20→        // at the X character on the right                       X
 21→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
 22→            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
 23→                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 24→            // do something
 25→        }
 26→    }
 27→    ```
 28→
 29→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 30→
 31→    ```kotlin
 32→    fun foo() {
 33→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 34→        // at the X character on the right                       X
 35→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 36→            // do something
 37→        }
 38→    }
 39→    ```
 40→
 41→Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 42→
 43→## Blank line before declarations
 44→
 45→Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.
 46→
 47→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 48→
 49→    ```kotlin
 50→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
 51→
 52→    class FooBar {
 53→        val foo2 = "foo2"
 54→        val foo3 = "foo3"
 55→
 56→        fun bar1() {
 57→           val foo4 = "foo4"
 58→           val foo5 = "foo5"
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        fun bar2() = "bar"
 62→
 63→        val foo6 = "foo3"
 64→        val foo7 = "foo4"
 65→
 66→        enum class Foo {}
 67→    }
 68→    ```
 69→
 70→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 71→
 72→    ```kotlin
 73→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
 74→    class FooBar {
 75→        val foo2 = "foo2"
 76→        val foo3 = "foo3"
 77→        fun bar1() {
 78→           val foo4 = "foo4"
 79→           val foo5 = "foo5"
 80→        }
 81→        fun bar2() = "bar"
 82→        val foo6 = "foo3"
 83→        val foo7 = "foo4"
 84→        enum class Foo {}
 85→    }
 86→    ```
 87→
 88→Rule id: `blank-line-before-declaration` (`standard` rule set)
 89→
 90→!!! Note
 91→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 92→
 93→## Discouraged comment location
 94→
 95→Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 96→
 97→!!! note
 98→    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier. 
 99→
100→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
101→
102→    ```kotlin
103→    fun <T> /* some comment */ foo(t: T) = "some-result"
104→
105→    fun foo() {
106→        if (true)
107→            // some comment
108→            bar()
109→    }
110→    ```
111→
112→Rule id: `discouraged-comment-location` (`standard` rule set)
113→
114→## Disallow empty lines at start of class body
115→
116→Detect blank lines at start of a class body.
117→
118→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
119→
120→    ```kotlin
121→    class Foo {
122→        val foo = "foo"
123→    }
124→    ```
125→
126→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
127→
128→    ```kotlin
129→    class Foo {
130→
131→        val foo = "foo"
132→    }
133→    ```
134→
135→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` (`standard` rule set)
136→
137→!!! Note
138→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
139→
140→## Disallow consecutive comments
141→
142→Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
143→- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
144→- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
145→- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
146→
147→Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
148→
149→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
150→
151→    ```kotlin
152→    // An EOL comment
153→    // may be followed by another EOL comment
154→    val foo = "foo"
155→
156→    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
157→
158→    /*
159→     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
160→     */
161→
162→    /**
163→      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
164→      */
165→    fun bar() = "bar"
166→
167→    ```
168→
169→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
170→
171→    ```kotlin
172→    /*
173→     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
174→     */
175→    /*
176→     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
177→     */
178→    val bar = "bar" 
179→
180→    /**
181→      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
182→      */
183→
184→    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
185→    ```
186→
187→Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
188→
189→!!! Note
190→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
191→
192→## Function signature
193→
194→Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
195→
196→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
197→
198→    ```kotlin
199→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
200→    // at the X character on the right           X
201→    fun foooooooo(
202→        a: Any,
203→        b: Any,
204→        c: Any
205→    ): String {
206→        // body
207→    }
208→
209→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
210→    // at the X character on the right           X
211→    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
212→        // body
213→    }
214→
215→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
216→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
217→    // at the X character on the right           X
218→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
219→        .uppercase()
220→
221→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
222→    // or 'always'.
223→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
224→    // at the X character on the right           X
225→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
226→        "some-result"
227→            .uppercase()
228→    ```
229→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
230→
231→    ```kotlin
232→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
233→    // at the X character on the right           X
234→    fun foooooooo(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
235→        // body
236→    }
237→
238→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
239→    // at the X character on the right           X
240→    fun bar(
241→        a: Any,
242→        b: Any,
243→        c: Any
244→    ): String {
245→        // body
246→    }
247→
248→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
249→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
250→    // at the X character on the right           X
251→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
252→        "some-result"
253→            .uppercase()
254→
255→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
256→    // or 'always'.
257→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
258→    // at the X character on the right           X
259→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
260→        .uppercase()
261→    ```
262→
263→Rule id: `function-signature` (`standard` rule set)
264→
265→## If else bracing
266→
267→If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
268→
269→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
270→
271→    ```kotlin
272→    fun foo(value: int) {
273→        if (value > 0) {
274→            doSomething()
275→        } else if (value < 0) {
276→            doSomethingElse()
277→        } else {
278→            doSomethingElse2()
279→        }
280→    }
281→    ```
282→
283→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
284→
285→    ```kotlin
286→    fun foo(value: int) {
287→        if (value > 0)
288→            doSomething()
289→        else if (value < 0) {
290→            doSomethingElse()
291→        } else
292→            doSomethingElse2()
293→    }
294→    ```
295→
296→Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
297→
298→!!! Note
299→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
300→
301→## If else wrapping
302→
303→A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
304→
305→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
306→
307→    ```kotlin
308→    fun foobar() {
309→        if (true) foo()
310→        if (true) foo() else bar()
311→    }
312→    ```
313→
314→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
315→
316→    ```kotlin
317→    fun foobar() {
318→        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
319→        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
320→        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
321→        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
322→    }
323→    ```
324→
325→Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
326→
327→!!! Note
328→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
329→
330→## Naming
331→
332→### Function naming
333→
334→Enforce naming of function. 
335→
336→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
337→
338→    ```kotlin
339→    fun foo() {}
340→    fun fooBar() {}
341→    ```
342→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
343→
344→    ```kotlin
345→    @Test
346→    fun `Some name`() {}
347→
348→    @Test
349→    fun do_something() {}
350→    ```
351→
352→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
353→
354→    ```kotlin
355→    fun Foo() {}
356→    fun Foo_Bar() {}
357→    fun `Some name`() {}
358→    fun do_something() {}
359→    ```
360→
361→!!! note
362→    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.
363→
364→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.
365→
366→Rule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)
367→
368→### Package naming
369→
370→Enforce naming of package.
371→
372→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PackageName`.
373→
374→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
375→
376→    ```kotlin
377→    package foo
378→    package foo.foo
379→    package foo_bar
380→    package foo.foo_bar
381→    ```
382→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
383→
384→    ```kotlin
385→    package Foo
386→    package foo.Foo
387→    package `foo bar`
388→    package foo.`foo bar`
389→    ```
390→
391→Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
392→
393→### Property naming
394→
395→Enforce naming of property.
396→
397→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
398→
399→    ```kotlin
400→    val FOO_1 = Foo()
401→    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
402→
403→    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
404→
405→    class Bar {
406→        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
407→        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
408→
409→        val foo2 = "foo"
410→        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
411→
412→        // Backing property
413→        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
414→        val elementList: List<Element>
415→            get() = _elementList
416→    }
417→    ```
418→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
419→
420→    ```kotlin
421→    val Foo1 = Foo()
422→    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
423→
424→    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
425→
426→    class Bar {
427→        const val foo2 = "foo"
428→        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
429→
430→        val FOO2 = "foo"
431→        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
432→
433→        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
434→        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
435→
436→        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
437→        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
438→        val elementList2: List<Element>
439→            get() = _elementList2
440→    }
441→    ```
442→
443→!!! note
444→    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
445→
446→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
447→
448→Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
449→
450→## No empty file
451→
452→A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.
453→
454→Rule id: `no-empty-file`
455→
456→## No single line block comments
457→
458→A single line block comment should be replaced with an EOL comment when possible.
459→
460→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
461→
462→    ```kotlin
463→    /*
464→     * Some comment
465→     */
466→    val foo = "foo" // Some comment
467→    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
468→
469→    /* ktlint-disable foo-rule-id bar-rule-id */
470→    val foo = "foo"
471→    /* ktlint-enable foo-rule-id bar-rule-id */
472→    ```
473→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
474→
475→    ```kotlin
476→    /* Some comment */
477→    val foo = "foo" /* Some comment */
478→    ```
479→
480→Rule id: `no-single-line-block-comment` (`standard` rule set)
481→
482→## Spacing
483→
484→### No blank lines in list
485→
486→Disallow blank lines to be used in lists before the first element, between elements, and after the last element.
487→
488→*Super type*
489→
490→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
491→
492→    ```kotlin
493→    class FooBar:
494→        Foo,
495→        Bar {
496→        // body
497→    }
498→    ```
499→
500→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
501→
502→    ```kotlin
503→    class FooBar:
504→
505→        Foo,
506→
507→        Bar
508→
509→    {
510→        // body
511→    }
512→    ```
513→
514→*Type argument list*
515→
516→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
517→
518→    ```kotlin
519→    val foobar: FooBar<
520→        Foo,
521→        Bar,
522→        > = FooBar(Foo(), Bar())
523→    ```
524→
525→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
526→
527→    ```kotlin
528→    val foobar: FooBar<
529→
530→        Foo,
531→
532→        Bar,
533→
534→        > = FooBar(Foo(), Bar())
535→    ```
536→
537→*Type constraint list*
538→
539→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
540→
541→    ```kotlin
542→    class BiAdapter<C : RecyclerView.ViewHolder, V1 : C, V2 : C, out A1, out A2>(
543→        val adapter1: A1,
544→        val adapter2: A2
545→    ) : RecyclerView.Adapter<C>()
546→        where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>, A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
547→              A2 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V2>, A2 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider {
548→        // body
549→    }
550→    ```
551→
552→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
553→
554→    ```kotlin
555→    class BiAdapter<C : RecyclerView.ViewHolder, V1 : C, V2 : C, out A1, out A2>(
556→        val adapter1: A1,
557→        val adapter2: A2
558→    ) : RecyclerView.Adapter<C>()
559→        where
560→              A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>, A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
561→
562→              A2 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V2>, A2 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider
563→    {
564→        // body
565→    }
566→    ```
567→
568→*Type parameter list*
569→
570→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
571→
572→    ```kotlin
573→    fun <
574→        Foo,
575→        Bar,
576→        > foobar()
577→    ```
578→
579→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
580→
581→    ```kotlin
582→    fun <
583→
584→        Foo,
585→
586→        Bar,
587→
588→        > foobar()
589→    ```
590→
591→*Value argument list*
592→
593→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
594→
595→    ```kotlin

599→    )
600→    ```
601→
602→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
603→
604→    ```kotlin

606→
607→        "foo",

641→
642→### Parameter list spacing
643→
644→Consistent spacing inside the parameter list.
645→
646→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
647→
648→    ```kotlin
649→    fun foo(a: Any ) = "some-result"
650→    fun foo() = "some-result"
651→    ```
652→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
653→
654→    ```kotlin
655→    fun foo( a : Any ) = "some-result"
656→    fun foo(
657→    ) = "some-result"
658→    ```
659→
660→Rule id: `parameter-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
661→
662→### String template indent
663→
664→Enforce consistent string template indentation for multiline string templates which are post-fixed with `.trimIndent()`. The opening and closing `"""` are placed on separate lines and the indentation of the content of the template is aligned with the `"""`.
665→
666→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
667→
668→    ```kotlin
669→    val foo =
670→        """
671→        line1
672→        line2
673→        """.trimIndent()
674→    fun foo() {
675→        // The opening """ can not be wrapped to next line as that would result in a compilation error
676→        return """
677→            line1
678→            line2
679→            """.trimIndent()
680→    }
681→    ```
682→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
683→
684→    ```kotlin
685→    val foo = """
686→              line1
687→              line2
688→              """.trimIndent()
689→    fun foo() {
690→        return """
691→            line1
692→            line2
693→        """.trimIndent()
694→    }
695→    ```
696→
697→Rule id: `string-template-indent` (`standard` rule set)
698→
699→!!! Note
700→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
701→
702→### Try catch finally spacing
703→
704→Enforce consistent spacing in `try { .. } catch { .. } finally { .. }`.
705→
706→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
707→
708→    ```kotlin
709→    fun foo() =
710→        try {
711→            // do something
712→        } catch (exception: Exception) {
713→            // handle exception
714→        } finally {
715→            // clean up
716→        }
717→    ```
718→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
719→
720→    ```kotlin
721→    fun foo1() = try { /* ... */ } catch (exception: Exception) { /* ... */ } finally { /* ... */ }
722→    fun foo2() = 
723→        try {
724→            // do something
725→        }
726→        catch (exception: Exception) {
727→            // handle exception
728→        }
729→        finally {
730→            // clean up
731→        }
732→    ```
733→
734→Rule id: `try-catch-finally-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
735→
736→!!! Note
737→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
738→
739→### Type argument list spacing
740→
741→Spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
742→
743→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
744→
745→    ```kotlin
746→    val res = ArrayList<LintError>()
747→    class B<T> : A<T>() {
748→        override fun x() = super<A>.x()
749→    }
750→    ```
751→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
752→
753→    ```kotlin
754→    val res = ArrayList < LintError > ()
755→    class B<T> : A< T >() {
756→        override fun x() = super< A >.x()
757→    }
758→    ```
759→
760→Rule id: `type-argument-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
761→
762→### Type parameter list spacing
763→
764→Spacing after a type parameter list in function and class declarations.
765→
766→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
767→
768→    ```kotlin
769→    fun <T> foo1(t: T) = "some-result"
770→    fun <T> foo2(t: T) = "some-result"
771→    fun <T> foo3(t: T) = "some-result"
772→    ```
773→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
774→
775→    ```kotlin
776→    fun<T> foo1(t: T) = "some-result"
777→    fun <T>foo2(t: T) = "some-result"
778→    fun<T>foo3(t: T) = "some-result"
779→    ```

781→Rule id: `type-parameter-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
782→
783→## Wrapping
784→
785→### Content receiver wrapping
786→
787→Wraps the content receiver list to a separate line regardless of maximum line length. If the maximum line length is configured and is exceeded, wrap the context receivers and if needed its projection types to separate lines.
788→
789→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
790→
791→    ```kotlin
792→    // ALways wrap regardless of whether max line length is set
793→    context(Foo)
794→    fun fooBar()
795→
796→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
797→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line
798→    context(
799→        Fooooooooooooooooooo1,
800→        Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2
801→    )
802→    fun fooBar()
803→
804→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
805→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line.
806→    // Also, wrap each of it projection types in case a context
807→    // receiver does not fit on a single line after it has been
808→    // wrapped.
809→    context(
810→        Foooooooooooooooo<
811→            Foo,
812→            Bar
813→            >
814→    )
815→    fun fooBar()
816→    ```
817→
818→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
819→
820→    ```kotlin
821→    // Should be wrapped regardless of whether max line length is set
822→    context(Foo) fun fooBar()
823→
824→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
825→    // fit on a single line
826→    context(Fooooooooooooooooooo1, Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2)
827→    fun fooBar()
828→
829→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
830→    // fit on a single line. Also, it should wrap each of it projection
831→    // type in case a context receiver does not fit on a single line 
832→    // after it has been wrapped.
833→    context(Foooooooooooooooo<Foo, Bar>)
834→    fun fooBar()
835→    ```
836→
837→Rule id: `context-receiver-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
838→
839→### Enum wrapping
840→
841→An enum should be a single line, or each enum entry has to be placed on a separate line. In case the enumeration contains enum entries and declarations those are to be separated by a blank line.
842→
843→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
844→
845→    ```kotlin
846→    enum class Foo { A, B, C, D }
847→
848→    enum class Foo {
849→        A,
850→        B,
851→        C,
852→        D,
853→        ;
854→
855→        fun foo() = "foo"
856→    }
857→    ```
858→
859→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
860→
861→    ```kotlin
862→    enum class Foo {
863→        A,
864→        B, C,
865→        D
866→    }
867→
868→    enum class Foo {
869→        A;
870→        fun foo() = "foo"
871→    }
872→    ```
873→
874→Rule id: `enum-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
875→
876→### Multiline expression wrapping
877→
878→Multiline expression on the right hand side of an expression are forced to start on a separate line. Expressions in return statement are excluded as that would result in a compilation error. 
879→
880→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
881→
882→    ```kotlin
883→    val foo =
884→        foo(
885→            parameterName =
886→                "The quick brown fox "
887→                    .plus("jumps ")
888→                    .plus("over the lazy dog"),
889→        )
890→    ```
891→
892→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
893→
894→    ```kotlin
895→    val foo = foo(
896→        parameterName = "The quick brown fox "
897→            .plus("jumps ")
898→            .plus("over the lazy dog"),
899→    )
900→    ```
901→
902→Rule id: `multiline-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
903→
904→!!! Note
905→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
906→
907→### Statement wrapping
908→
909→A function, class/object body or other block body statement has to be placed on different line than the braces of the body block.
910→
911→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
912→
913→    ```kotlin
914→    fun foo() {
915→        if (true) {
916→            // do something
917→        }
918→    }
919→    class A {
920→        val a = 0
921→        val b = 1
922→    }
923→    ```
924→
925→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
926→
927→    ```kotlin
928→    fun foo() { if (true) {
929→            // do something
930→        }
931→    }
932→    class A { val a = 0
933→        val b = 1 }
934→    ```
935→
936→Rule id: `statement-wrapping`
937→

{"file_path": "/project/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md", "old_string": "## If else bracing", "new_string": "## Ktlint suppression

Detects and replaces the deprecated `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with `@Suppress` annotations.

The `ktlint-disable` directive in EOL comments:
```kotlin
val foo = "foo" // ktlint-disable some-rule-id
```
is replaced with:
```kotlin
@Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule-id")
val foo = "foo"
```

The block `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives:
```kotlin
/* ktlint-disable some-rule-id */
val foo = "foo"
/* ktlint-enable some-rule-id */
```
are replaced with a `@Suppress` annotation when a single construct is enclosed:
```kotlin
@Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule-id")
val foo = "foo"
```

A `ktlint-disable` block comment at the start of the file (before the package declaration) without a matching `ktlint-enable` directive is replaced with a `@file:Suppress` annotation.

!!! note
    This rule itself can not be suppressed. This ensures that deprecated directives will always be reported and/or replaced with `@Suppress` annotations.

Rule id: `ktlint-suppression` (`standard` rule set)

## If else bracing"}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
20→
21→/**
22→ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23→ */
24→public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25→    StandardRule(
26→        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28→            setOf(
29→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31→            ),
32→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33→    ),
34→    Rule.Experimental,
35→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37→        node: ASTNode,
38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40→    ) {
41→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42→            val afterBlockComment =
43→                node
44→                    .leaves()
45→                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46→                    .firstOrNull()
47→                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48→
49→            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50→                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51→                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52→            ) {
53→                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54→                if (autoCorrect) {
55→                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56→                }
57→            }
58→        }
59→    }
60→
61→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65→        rawRemove()
66→    }
67→
68→    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69→
70→    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71→    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72→        this
73→            ?.text
74→            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75→            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76→            ?.trim()
77→            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78→            ?: false
79→}
80→
81→public val NO_SINGLE_LINE_BLOCK_COMMENT_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule().ruleId
82→

content.thinking:
The TODO comment says "Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported". Since we're now deprecating the directive but still need backward compatibility during the transition, we should keep this check. The check prevents the `no-single-line-block-comment` rule from trying to replace ktlint suppression directives with EOL comments.

However, since we now have the KtlintSuppressionRule which will handle these directives, we should make sure the NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule runs after the KtlintSuppressionRule so that:
1. First, the KtlintSuppressionRule replaces the ktlint-disable/enable directives with @Suppress annotations
2. Then, the NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule won't encounter these directives anymore

But for backward compatibility, we should keep the check in case someone hasn't enabled the KtlintSuppressionRule.

Let me update the comment to be more accurate about the current state.

2. Modified `SuppressHandler` - Added logic to prevent the `ktlint-suppression` rule from being suppressed. This ensures that the rule always runs, even when code is suppressed with `@Suppress("ktlint")`.

The implementation handles:- EOL comments like `// ktlint-disable` → `@Suppress`
- Block comments like `/* ktlint-disable */ ... /* ktlint-enable */` → `@Suppress`
- File-level suppressions at the start of a file → `@file:Suppress`
- Multiple rule ids
- Qualified rule ids (with rule set prefix)
- Unqualified rule ids (which get prefixed with `standard:`)

content.text:
All tests pass and everything compiles correctly. Let me provide a summary of the changes I've made to implement the feature to drop support for `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` directives:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Created new `KtlintSuppressionRule` 
**File:** `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/KtlintSuppressionRule.kt`

This experimental rule detects and replaces deprecated `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with `@Suppress` annotations:
- EOL comments like `val foo = "foo" // ktlint-disable some-rule` are replaced with `@Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule")`
- Block comments like `/* ktlint-disable */ ... /* ktlint-enable */` are replaced with `@Suppress` annotations when a single statement is enclosed
- File-level suppression directives at the start of the file are converted to `@file:Suppress` annotations
- Unqualified rule ids are automatically prefixed with `standard:`

### 2. Made the rule unsuppressible
**File:** `/project/ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/SuppressHandler.kt`

Added logic to prevent the `ktlint-suppression` rule from being suppressed, even when `@Suppress("ktlint")` is used. This ensures deprecated directives are always reported and can be auto-corrected.

### 3. Registered the rule in StandardRuleSetProvider
**File:** `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/StandardRuleSetProvider.kt`

Added `KtlintSuppressionRule()` to the set of rule providers.

### 4. Updated NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule
**File:** `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule.kt`

Updated the TODO comment to clarify the current state of the ktlint suppression directive support.

### 5. Updated FAQ documentation
**File:** `/project/documentation/snapshot/docs/faq.md`

Updated to indicate that EOL and block comment suppression directives are deprecated and will be removed in a future version.

### 6. Added experimental rule documentation
**File:** `/project/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`

Added documentation for the new `ktlint-suppression` rule explaining its purpose and behavior.

## How to Use

Users can enable the rule to automatically migrate their code:
```editorconfig
ktlint_standard_ktlint-suppression = enabled
```

Or enable all experimental rules:
```editorconfig
ktlint_experimental = enabled
```

 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.SuppressionLocatorBuilder.CommentSuppressionHint.Type.BLOCK_START
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.SuppressionLocatorBuilder.CommentSuppressionHint.Type.EOL

 13→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotated
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.ValueArgument
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.endOffset
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.startOffset
 21→
 22→/**
 23→ * Detects if given `ruleId` at given `offset` is suppressed.
 24→ */
 25→internal typealias SuppressionLocator = (offset: Int, ruleId: RuleId) -> Boolean
 26→
 27→internal object SuppressionLocatorBuilder {
 28→    /**
 29→     * No suppression is detected. Always returns `false`.
 30→     */
 31→    private val NO_SUPPRESSION: SuppressionLocator = { _, _ -> false }
 32→
 33→    private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 34→
 35→    /**
 36→     * Mapping of non-ktlint annotations to ktlint-annotation so that ktlint rules will be suppressed automatically
 37→     * when specific non-ktlint annotations are found. The prevents that developers have to specify multiple annotations
 38→     * for the same violation.
 39→     */
 40→    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP =
 41→        mapOf(
 42→            // It would have been nice if the official rule id's as defined in the Rules themselves could have been used here. But that would
 43→            // introduce a circular dependency between the ktlint-rule-engine and the ktlint-ruleset-standard modules.
 44→            "EnumEntryName" to RuleId("standard:enum-entry-name-case"),
 45→            "RemoveCurlyBracesFromTemplate" to RuleId("standard:string-template"),
 46→            "ClassName" to RuleId("standard:class-naming"),
 47→            "FunctionName" to RuleId("standard:function-naming"),
 48→            "PackageName" to RuleId("standard:package-name"),
 49→            "PropertyName" to RuleId("standard:property-naming"),
 50→        )
 51→    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS = setOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings")
 52→    private val SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID = RuleId("ktlint:suppress-all-rules")
 53→
 54→    /**
 55→     * Builds [SuppressionLocator] for given [rootNode] of AST tree.
 56→     */
 57→    fun buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(
 58→        rootNode: ASTNode,
 59→        editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 60→    ): SuppressionLocator {
 61→        val hintsList = collect(rootNode, FormatterTags.from(editorConfig))
 62→        return if (hintsList.isEmpty()) {
 63→            NO_SUPPRESSION
 64→        } else {
 65→            toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList)
 66→        }
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    private fun toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList: List<SuppressionHint>): SuppressionLocator =
 70→        { offset, ruleId ->
 71→            hintsList
 72→                .filter { offset in it.range }
 73→                .any { hint -> hint.disabledRuleIds.isEmpty() || hint.disabledRuleIds.contains(ruleId) }
 74→        }
 75→
 76→    private fun collect(
 77→        rootNode: ASTNode,
 78→        formatterTags: FormatterTags,
 79→    ): List<SuppressionHint> {
 80→        val suppressionHints = ArrayList<SuppressionHint>()
 81→        val commentSuppressionsHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
 82→        rootNode.collect { node ->
 83→            node
 84→                .takeIf { it is PsiComment }
 85→                ?.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags)
 86→                ?.let { commentSuppressionsHints.add(it) }
 87→
 88→            // Extract all Suppress annotations and create SuppressionHints
 89→            node
 90→                .psi
 91→                .safeAs<KtAnnotated>()
 92→                ?.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations()
 93→                ?.let { suppressionHints.add(it) }
 94→        }
 95→
 96→        return suppressionHints.plus(
 97→            commentSuppressionsHints.toSuppressionHints(rootNode),
 98→        )
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun ASTNode.collect(block: (node: ASTNode) -> Unit) {
102→        block(this)
103→        this
104→            .getChildren(null)
105→            .forEach { it.collect(block) }
106→    }
107→
108→    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
109→        if (text.startsWith("//")) {
110→            createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags)
111→        } else {
112→            createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags)
113→        }
114→
115→    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
116→        text
117→            .removePrefix("//")
118→            .trim()
119→            .split(" ")
120→            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
121→            ?.takeIf { it[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || it[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff }
122→            ?.let { parts ->
123→                CommentSuppressionHint(
124→                    this,
125→                    HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
126→                    EOL,
127→                )
128→            }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
131→        text
132→            .removePrefix("/*")
133→            .removeSuffix("*/")
134→            .trim()
135→            .split(" ")
136→            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
137→            ?.let { parts ->
138→                if (parts[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff) {
139→                    CommentSuppressionHint(
140→                        this,
141→                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
142→                        BLOCK_START,
143→                    )
144→                } else if (parts[0] == KTLINT_ENABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOn) {
145→                    CommentSuppressionHint(
146→                        this,
147→                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
148→                        BLOCK_END,
149→                    )
150→                } else {
151→                    null
152→                }
153→            }
154→
155→    private fun MutableList<CommentSuppressionHint>.toSuppressionHints(rootNode: ASTNode): MutableList<SuppressionHint> {
156→        val suppressionHints = mutableListOf<SuppressionHint>()
157→        val blockCommentSuppressionHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
158→        forEach { commentSuppressionHint ->
159→            when (commentSuppressionHint.type) {
160→                EOL -> {
161→                    val commentNode = commentSuppressionHint.node
162→                    suppressionHints.add(
163→                        SuppressionHint(
164→                            IntRange(commentNode.prevNewLineOffset(), commentNode.startOffset),
165→                            commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
166→                        ),
167→                    )
168→                }
169→
170→                BLOCK_START -> {
171→                    blockCommentSuppressionHints.add(commentSuppressionHint)
172→                }
173→
174→                BLOCK_END -> {
175→                    // match open hint
176→                    Unit
177→                    blockCommentSuppressionHints
178→                        .lastOrNull { it.disabledRuleIds == commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds }
179→                        ?.let { openHint ->
180→                            blockCommentSuppressionHints.remove(openHint)
181→                            suppressionHints.add(
182→                                SuppressionHint(
183→                                    IntRange(openHint.node.startOffset, commentSuppressionHint.node.startOffset - 1),
184→                                    commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
185→                                ),
186→                            )
187→                        }
188→                }
189→            }
190→        }
191→        suppressionHints.addAll(
192→            blockCommentSuppressionHints.map {
193→                SuppressionHint(
194→                    IntRange(it.node.startOffset, rootNode.textLength),
195→                    it.disabledRuleIds,
196→                )
197→            },
198→        )
199→        return suppressionHints
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.prevNewLineOffset(): Int =
203→        prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
204→            ?.let { it.startOffset + it.text.lastIndexOf('\n') + 1 }
205→            ?: 0
206→
207→    private fun List<String>.tailToRuleIds() = tail().mapNotNull { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
208→
209→    private fun <T> List<T>.tail() = this.subList(1, this.size)
210→
211→    /**
212→     * Creates [SuppressionHint] from annotations of given [PsiElement]. Returns null if no targetAnnotations are
213→     * present or no mapping exists between annotations' values and ktlint rules
214→     */
215→    private fun PsiElement.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations(): SuppressionHint? =
216→        (this as? KtAnnotated)?.let { ktAnnotated ->
217→            ktAnnotated
218→                .annotationEntries
219→                .filter {
220→                    it.calleeExpression
221→                        ?.constructorReferenceExpression
222→                        ?.getReferencedName() in SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS
223→                }.flatMap(KtAnnotationEntry::getValueArguments)
224→                .mapNotNull { it.toRuleId(SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP) }
225→                .let { suppressedRuleIds ->
226→                    when {
227→                        suppressedRuleIds.isEmpty() -> null
228→                        suppressedRuleIds.contains(SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID) ->
229→                            SuppressionHint(
230→                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
231→                                emptySet(),
232→                            )
233→
234→                        else ->
235→                            SuppressionHint(
236→                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
237→                                suppressedRuleIds.toSet(),
238→                            )
239→                    }
240→                }
241→        }
242→
243→    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244→        getArgumentExpression()
245→            ?.text
246→            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")
247→            ?.let { argumentExpressionText ->
248→                when {
249→                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250→                        // Disable all rules
251→                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252→                    }
253→                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254→                        // Disable specific rule
255→                        argumentExpressionText
256→                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257→                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
258→                    }
259→                    else -> {
260→                        // Disable specific rule if the annotation value is mapped to a specific rule
261→                        annotationValueToRuleMapping[argumentExpressionText]
262→                    }
263→                }
264→            }
265→
266→    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =
267→        try {
268→            // For backwards compatibility the suppression hints have to be prefixed with the standard rule set id when the rule id is
269→            // not prefixed with any rule set id.
270→            RuleId
271→                .prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(ruleId)
272→                .let { RuleId(it) }
273→        } catch (illegalArgumentException: IllegalArgumentException) {
274→            // Ktlint should not terminate with an exception in case the code being scanned contains a suppression for a non-existing rule.
275→            // Instead, a warning should be printed and the invalid reference is to be ignored. The original ruleId is printed in the
276→            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an
277→            // unqualified ruleId.
278→            LOGGER.warn {
279→                """
280→                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281→                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}
282→                """.trimIndent()
283→            }
284→            null
285→        }
286→
287→    /**
288→     * @param range zero-based range of lines where lint errors should be suppressed
289→     * @param disabledRuleIds empty set means "all"
290→     */
291→    private data class SuppressionHint(
292→        val range: IntRange,
293→        val disabledRuleIds: Set<RuleId> = emptySet(),
294→    )
295→
296→    private data class CommentSuppressionHint(
297→        val node: ASTNode,
298→        val disabledRuleIds: Set<RuleId> = emptySet(),
299→        val type: Type,
300→    ) {
301→        enum class Type {
302→            EOL,
303→            BLOCK_START,
304→            BLOCK_END,
305→        }
306→    }
307→
308→    private const val KTLINT_DISABLE = "ktlint-disable"
309→    private const val KTLINT_ENABLE = "ktlint-enable"
310→}

 6→/**
 7→ * Gets the rule provider for the [KtLintRuleEngine] by applying the [ruleFilters] in the given order on the set of [RuleProvider]s provided
 8→ * by the previous (or the initial list of [RuleProvider]s).
 9→ */
10→internal fun KtLintRuleEngine.ruleProviders(vararg ruleFilters: RuleFilter): Set<RuleProvider> {
11→    var ruleProviders = initialRuleProviders()
12→    val ruleFilterIterator = ruleFilters.iterator()
13→    while (ruleFilterIterator.hasNext()) {
14→        val ruleFilter = ruleFilterIterator.next()
15→        ruleProviders = ruleFilter.filter(ruleProviders)
16→    }
17→    return ruleProviders
18→}
19→
20→private fun KtLintRuleEngine.initialRuleProviders() =
21→    ruleProviders
22→        .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
23→        .toSet()

26→    fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider>
27→}

 29→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 30→    val code: Code,
 31→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 32→    val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
 33→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 34→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 35→) {
 36→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 37→
 38→    init {
 39→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 43→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode, editorConfig)
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    fun executeRule(
 47→        rule: Rule,
 48→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 49→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 50→    ) {
 51→        try {
 52→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 53→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 54→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 55→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 56→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 57→                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
 58→                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
 59→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),
 60→            )
 61→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→            rule.afterLastNode()
 63→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 64→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 65→                e.line,
 66→                e.col,
 67→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 68→                """
 69→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 70→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 71→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 72→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 73→                """.trimIndent(),
 74→                e.cause,
 75→            )
 76→        }
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        rule: Rule,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        /**
 86→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 87→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 88→         */
 89→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 91→            try {
 92→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 93→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→                }
 95→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 96→                    node
 97→                        .getChildren(null)
 98→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 99→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
100→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
101→                                    childNode,
102→                                    rule,
103→                                    autoCorrect,
104→                                    emit,
105→                                )
106→                            }
107→                        }
108→                }
109→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
110→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
111→                }
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                if (autoCorrect) {
114→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
115→                    // original AST
116→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
117→                        rule,
118→                        0,
119→                        0,
120→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
121→                        e.cause ?: e,
122→                    )
123→                } else {
124→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
125→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
126→                        rule,
127→                        line,
128→                        col,
129→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
130→                        e.cause ?: e,
131→                    )
132→                }
133→            }
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    companion object {
138→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
139→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
140→            code: Code,
141→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
142→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
143→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
144→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
145→
146→            val psiFileName =
147→                code
148→                    .filePath
149→                    ?.pathString
150→                    ?: if (code.script) {
151→                        "File.kts"
152→                    } else {
153→                        "File.kt"
154→                    }
155→            val psiFile =
156→                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
157→                    psiFileName,
158→                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
159→                    normalizedText,
160→                ) as KtFile
161→            psiFile
162→                .findErrorElement()
163→                ?.let { errorElement ->
164→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
165→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
166→                }
167→
168→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
169→
170→            val editorConfig =
171→                ktLintRuleEngine
172→                    .editorConfigLoader
173→                    .load(code.filePath)
174→                    .also {
175→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
176→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
177→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
178→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
179→                    }
180→
181→            val ruleProviders =
182→                ktLintRuleEngine
183→                    .ruleProviders(
184→                        RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
185→                        RunAfterRuleFilter(),
186→                    )
187→
188→            return RuleExecutionContext(
189→                code,
190→                rootNode,
191→                ruleProviders,
192→                editorConfig,
193→                positionInTextLocator,
194→            )
195→        }
196→
197→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
198→            return text
199→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
200→                .replace("\r", "\n")
201→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
202→        }
203→
204→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
205→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
206→                return this
207→            }
208→            this.children.forEach { child ->
209→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
210→                if (errorElement != null) {
211→                    return errorElement
212→                }
213→            }
214→            return null
215→        }
216→    }
217→}
218→
219→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
220→    propertyName: String,
221→    ktlintVersion: String,
222→) {
223→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
224→        LOGGER.warn {
225→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
226→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
227→        }
228→    }
229→}
230→
231→private class RuleExecutionException(
232→    val rule: Rule,
233→    val line: Int,
234→    val col: Int,
235→    override val cause: Throwable,
236→) : Throwable(cause)

 36→public class KtLintRuleEngine(
 37→    /**
 38→     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39→     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40→     */
 41→    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42→    /**
 43→     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 44→     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45→     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46→     */
 47→    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48→    /**
 49→     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50→     * property being set in any other way.
 51→     */
 52→    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→    /**
 54→     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55→     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56→     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57→     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58→     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59→     */
 60→    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61→    /**
 62→     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63→     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64→     */
 65→    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66→    /**
 67→     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68→     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69→     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70→     * is included.
 71→     */
 72→    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73→) {
 74→    init {
 75→        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 76→            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81→
 82→    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83→        EditorConfigLoader(
 84→            fileSystem,
 85→            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86→            editorConfigDefaults,
 87→            editorConfigOverride,
 88→        )
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92→     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93→     *
 94→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96→     */
 97→    public fun lint(
 98→        code: Code,
 99→        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100→    ) {
101→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102→
103→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105→
106→        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107→            .visitor()
108→            .invoke { rule ->
109→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111→                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112→                }
113→            }
114→
115→        errors
116→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118→
119→        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120→    }
121→
122→    /**
123→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125→     *
126→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
128→     */
129→    public fun format(
130→        code: Code,
131→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132→    ): String {
133→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134→
135→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
137→
138→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
139→        var formatRunCount = 0
140→        var mutated: Boolean
141→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
142→        do {
143→            mutated = false
144→            formatRunCount++
145→            visitorProvider
146→                .visitor()
147→                .invoke { rule ->
148→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
149→                        if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
150→                            mutated = true
151→                            /**
152→                             * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
153→                             * suppression hints might have changed.
154→                             */
155→                            ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
156→                        }
157→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
158→                        errors.add(
159→                            Pair(
160→                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
161→                                // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
162→                                // does correct the error.
163→                                canBeAutoCorrected,
164→                            ),
165→                        )
166→                        // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be different
167→                        // from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it cn be helpful to know the exact order in which
168→                        // violations are being solved.
169→                        LOGGER.trace {
170→                            "Format violation: ${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
171→                                canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
172→                        }
173→                    }
174→                }
175→        } while (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE)
176→        if (formatRunCount == MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE && mutated) {
177→            LOGGER.warn {
178→                "Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file " +
179→                    "${code.filePathOrStdin()} in $MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE consecutive runs of format."
180→            }
181→        }
182→
183→        errors
184→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
185→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
186→
187→        if (!mutated && formatRunCount == 1) {
188→            return code.content
189→        }
190→
191→        val formattedCode =
192→            ruleExecutionContext
193→                .rootNode
194→                .text
195→                .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
196→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
197→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
198→        } else {
199→            formattedCode
200→        }.also {
201→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
206→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
207→        return when {
208→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
209→            else -> "\n"
210→        }
211→    }

213→    /**
214→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
215→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
216→     *
217→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
218→     * ```properties
219→     * final-newline=true
220→     * indent-size=4
221→     * ```
222→     */
223→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
224→        val codeStyle =
225→            editorConfigOverride
226→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
227→                ?.parsed
228→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
229→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
230→        val rules =
231→            ruleProviders
232→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
233→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
234→                .toSet()
235→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
236→            .generateEditorconfig(
237→                rules,
238→                codeStyle,
239→                filePath,
240→            )
241→    }
242→
243→    /**
244→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
245→     */
246→    public fun trimMemory() {
247→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
248→    }
249→
250→    /**
251→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
252→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
253→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
254→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
255→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
256→     */
257→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
258→
259→    /**
260→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
261→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
262→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
263→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
264→     */
265→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
266→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
267→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
268→        )
269→    }
270→
271→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
272→
273→    public companion object {
274→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
275→
276→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
277→
278→        private const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
279→    }
280→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Provides a [Rule] instance. Important: to ensure that a [Rule] can keep internal state and that processing of files is thread-safe, a
 8→ * *new* instance should be provided on each call of the [provider] function.
 9→ */
10→public class RuleProvider private constructor(
11→    /**
12→     * Lambda which creates a new instance of the rule.
13→     */
14→    private val provider: () -> Rule,
15→    /**
16→     * The rule id of the [Rule] created by the provider.
17→     */
18→    public val ruleId: RuleId,
19→    /**
20→     * Flag whether the [Rule] created by the provider has to run as late as possible.
21→     */
22→    public val runAsLateAsPossible: Boolean,
23→    /**
24→     * The list of rules which have to run before the [Rule] created by the provider can be run.
25→     */
26→    public val runAfterRules: List<Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule>,
27→) {
28→    /**
29→     * Creates a new [Rule] instance.
30→     */
31→    public fun createNewRuleInstance(): Rule {
32→        return provider()
33→    }
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Lambda which creates a new instance of the [Rule]. Important: to ensure that a [Rule] can keep internal state and that processing of
37→     * files is thread-safe, a *new* instance should be provided on each call of the [provider] function.
38→     */
39→    public companion object {
40→        // Note that the KDOC is placed on the companion object to make it actually visually when the RuleProvider identifier is being
41→        // hovered in IntelliJ IDEA
42→        public operator fun invoke(provider: () -> Rule): RuleProvider =
43→            provider()
44→                .let { rule ->
45→                    RuleProvider(
46→                        provider = provider,
47→                        ruleId = rule.ruleId,
48→                        runAsLateAsPossible =
49→                            rule
50→                                .visitorModifiers
51→                                .filterIsInstance<Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible>()
52→                                .any(),
53→                        runAfterRules =
54→                            rule
55→                                .visitorModifiers
56→                                .filterIsInstance<Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule>(),
57→                    )
58→                }
59→    }
60→}
61→
62→/**
63→ * Extract all [PropertyType]'s for [EditorConfigProperty]'s that are in rules provided via the given collection of [RuleProvider]'s.
64→ */
65→public fun Collection<RuleProvider>.propertyTypes(): Set<PropertyType<*>> =
66→    map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
67→        .flatMap { it.usesEditorConfigProperties }
68→        .distinct()
69→        .toSet()
70→        .map { it.type }
71→        .toSet()
