1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.internal.IdNamingPolicy
  6→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  7→
  8→public data class RuleId(public val value: String) {
  9→    init {
 10→        IdNamingPolicy.enforceRuleIdNaming(value)
 11→    }
 12→
 13→    public val ruleSetId: RuleSetId
 14→        get() = RuleSetId(value.substringBefore(DELIMITER, ""))
 15→
 16→    public companion object {
 17→        private const val DELIMITER = ":"
 18→
 19→        // TODO: Remove in 1.0 version when backward compatibility of rule id references can be dropped.
 20→        public fun prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(id: String): String =
 21→            if (id.contains(DELIMITER)) {
 22→                id
 23→            } else {
 24→                "${RuleSetId.STANDARD.value}$DELIMITER$id"
 25→            }
 26→    }
 27→}
 28→
 29→public data class RuleSetId(public val value: String) {
 30→    init {
 31→        IdNamingPolicy.enforceRuleSetIdNaming(value)
 32→    }
 33→
 34→    public companion object {
 35→        /**
 36→         * The `standard` rule set is reserved for rules published by the KtLint project only. Custom rules should be provided via a rule
 37→         * set using a custom id so that in case of problems, it can be more clearly communicated to users which project is responsible for
 38→         * maintenance of the rule (set).
 39→         */
 40→        public val STANDARD: RuleSetId = RuleSetId("standard")
 41→    }
 42→}
 43→
 44→/**
 45→ * The [Rule] contains the life cycle hooks which are called by the KtLint rule engine to execute the rule.
 46→ *
 47→ * The implementation of a [Rule] **doesn't** have to be thread-safe or stateless provided that the [RuleProvider] creates a new instance of
 48→ * [Rule] on each call to [RuleProvider.provider].
 49→ *
 50→ * When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the [ruleId] and [about] fields, so that it is
 51→ * clear to users whenever they used the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint
 52→ * project.
 53→ */
 54→public open class Rule(
 55→    /**
 56→     * Identification of the rule. A [ruleId] has a value that must adhere the convention "<rule-set-id>:<rule-id>". The rule set id
 57→     * 'standard' is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API
 58→     * Consumers should use a prefix other than 'standard' to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
 59→     */
 60→    public open val ruleId: RuleId,
 61→    /**
 62→     * About the rule. Background information about the rule and its maintainer. About information is meant to be used in stack traces or
 63→     * API consumers to provide more detailed information about the rule.
 64→     */
 65→    public open val about: About,
 66→    /**
 67→     * Set of modifiers of the visitor. Preferably a rule has no modifiers at all, meaning that it is completely
 68→     * independent of all other rules.
 69→     */
 70→    public open val visitorModifiers: Set<VisitorModifier> = emptySet(),
 71→    /**
 72→     * Set of [EditorConfigProperty]'s that are to provided to the rule. Only specify the properties that are actually used by the rule.
 73→     */
 74→    public open val usesEditorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = emptySet(),
 75→) {
 76→    private var traversalState = TraversalState.NOT_STARTED
 77→
 78→    /**
 79→     * This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule
 80→     * before processing of nodes starts.
 81→     */
 82→    public open fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {}
 83→
 84→    /**
 85→     * This method is called on a node in AST before visiting the child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the
 86→     * child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST.
 87→     *
 88→     * @param node AST node
 89→     * @param autoCorrect indicates whether rule should attempt autocorrection
 90→     * @param emit a way for rule to notify about a violation (lint error)
 91→     */
 92→    public open fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96→    ) {}
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited.
100→     */
101→    @Suppress("unused")
102→    public open fun afterVisitChildNodes(
103→        node: ASTNode,
104→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
105→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
106→    ) {}
107→
108→    /**
109→     * This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state
110→     * of the rule.
111→     */
112→    public open fun afterLastNode() {}
113→
114→    /**
115→     * Checks whether the [Rule] instance is used for traversal of the AST and as of that potentially has changed the state of the [Rule]
116→     * provided that it has state.
117→     */
118→    public fun isUsedForTraversalOfAST(): Boolean = traversalState != TraversalState.NOT_STARTED
119→
120→    /**
121→     * Marks the [Rule] instance as being used for traversal of an AST. From this moment on, this instance of the [Rule]
122→     * can not be used to start a new traversal of the same or another AST as the instance might contain state.
123→     */
124→    public fun startTraversalOfAST() {
125→        require(traversalState == TraversalState.NOT_STARTED)
126→        traversalState = TraversalState.CONTINUE
127→    }
128→
129→    /**
130→     * Checks whether the next node in the AST is to be traversed. By default, the entire AST is traversed.
131→     */
132→    public fun shouldContinueTraversalOfAST(): Boolean = traversalState == TraversalState.CONTINUE
133→
134→    /**
135→     * Stops traversal of the AST. Intended usage it to prevent parsing of the remainder of the AST once the goal of the
136→     * rule is achieved. For example, if the ".editorconfig" property indent_size is set to 0 or -1 then the indent rule
137→     * should be disabled.
138→     *
139→     * When called in [beforeFirstNode], no AST nodes will be visited. [afterLastNode] is still called.
140→     *
141→     * When called in [beforeVisitChildNodes], the child nodes of that node will not be visited. [afterVisitChildNodes]
142→     * is still called for the node and each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally
143→     * [afterLastNode] is called.
144→     *
145→     * When called in [afterVisitChildNodes] the child nodes of that node are already visited. [afterVisitChildNodes] is
146→     * still called for each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally [afterLastNode] is
147→     * called.
148→     *
149→     * Calling in [afterLastNode] has no effect as traversal of the AST has already been completed.
150→     */
151→    public fun stopTraversalOfAST() {
152→        traversalState = TraversalState.STOP
153→    }
154→
155→    private enum class TraversalState {
156→        /**
157→         * Traversal of the AST is not started. As no life cycle hooks of the [Rule] have been executed, the [Rule]
158→         * instance can not contain state specific for the AST.
159→         */
160→        NOT_STARTED,
161→
162→        /**
163→         * Traversal of the AST is started and should be continued with next node.
164→         */
165→        CONTINUE,
166→
167→        /**
168→         * Stops traversal of yet unvisited nodes in the AST. See [stopTraversalOfAST] for more details.
169→         */
170→        STOP,
171→    }
172→
173→    /**
174→     * About the rule. Background information about the rule and its maintainer. About information is meant to be used in stack traces or
175→     * API consumers to provide more detailed information about the rule. Please provide all details below, so that users of your rule set
176→     * can easily get up-to-date information about the rule.
177→     */
178→    public data class About(
179→        /**
180→         * Name of person, organisation or group maintaining the rule.
181→         */
182→        val maintainer: String = "Not specified (and not maintained by the Ktlint project)",
183→        /**
184→         * Url to the repository containing the rule.
185→         */
186→        val repositoryUrl: String = "Not specified",
187→        /**
188→         * Url to the issue tracker of the project which provides the rule.
189→         */
190→        val issueTrackerUrl: String = "Not specified",
191→    )
192→
193→    public sealed class VisitorModifier {
194→        /**
195→         * Defines that the [Rule] that declares this [VisitorModifier] will be run after the [Rule] with rule id
196→         * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId].
197→         */
198→        public data class RunAfterRule(
199→            /**
200→             * The [RuleId] of the [Rule] which should run before the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
201→             */
202→            val ruleId: RuleId,
203→            /**
204→             * The [Mode] determines whether the [Rule] that declares this [VisitorModifier] can be run in case the [Rule] with rule id
205→             * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is not loaded or enabled.
206→             */
207→            val mode: Mode,
208→        ) : VisitorModifier() {
209→            public enum class Mode {
210→                /**
211→                 * Run the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule] regardless whether the [Rule] with ruleId
212→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is loaded or disabled. However, if that other rule is loaded and enabled, it runs
213→                 * before the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
214→                 */
215→                REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
216→
217→                /**
218→                 * Run the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule] only in case the [Rule] with ruleId
219→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is loaded *and* enabled. That other rule runs before the [Rule] that declares the
220→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
221→                 */
222→                ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
223→            }
224→        }
225→
226→        public object RunAsLateAsPossible : VisitorModifier()
227→    }
228→
229→    /**
230→     * This interface marks a rule as an 'experimental' rule. A rule marked with this interface will only be executed by ktlint in case the
231→     * '.editorconfig' allows this rule specifically or all experimental rules. This interface is used by Ktlint internally but is also
232→     * explicitly meant to be used by custom rule providers.
233→     */
234→    public interface Experimental
235→
236→    /**
237→     * This interface marks a rule as an Official rule. A rule marked with this interface will only be executed when by ktlint in case the
238→     * '.editorconfig' contains property "code_style = ktlint_official" or when enabled explicitly. This interface is intended to be used
239→     * in Ktlint internally only. It may be subject to change at any time without providing any backward compatibility.
240→     */
241→    public interface OfficialCodeStyle
242→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 4→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 5→
 6→internal class SuppressHandler(
 7→    private val suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator,
 8→    private val autoCorrect: Boolean,
 9→    private val emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
10→) {
11→    fun handle(
12→        node: ASTNode,
13→        ruleId: RuleId,
14→        function: (Boolean, (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit) -> Unit,
15→    ) {
16→        val suppress =
17→            suppressionLocator(
18→                node.startOffset,
19→                ruleId,
20→            )
21→        val autoCorrect = this.autoCorrect && !suppress
22→        val emit =
23→            if (suppress) {
24→                SUPPRESS_EMIT
25→            } else {
26→                this.emit
27→            }
28→        function(autoCorrect, emit)
29→    }
30→
31→    private companion object {
32→        // Swallow violation so that it is not emitted
33→        val SUPPRESS_EMIT: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _, _ -> }
34→    }
35→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RuleExecution
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName
 15→
 16→/**
 17→ * Filters the [RuleProvider]s defined in the [KtLintRuleEngine] for [Rule]s which are enabled for the given [EditorConfig].
 18→ */
 19→internal class RuleExecutionRuleFilter(
 20→    private val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 21→) : RuleFilter {
 22→    override fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider> {
 23→        val ruleExecutionFilter =
 24→            RuleExecutionFilter(
 25→                editorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders),
 26→                editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 27→            )
 28→        return ruleProviders
 29→            .filter { ruleExecutionFilter.isEnabled(it) }
 30→            .toSet()
 31→    }
 32→
 33→    private fun EditorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Map<String, RuleExecution> {
 34→        val ruleExecutionPropertyNames =
 35→            ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders)
 36→                .plus(ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders))
 37→                .plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name)
 38→        return map { it.name to RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE.parse(it.sourceValue).parsed }
 39→            .toMap()
 40→            .filterValues { it != null }
 41→            .filterKeys { it in ruleExecutionPropertyNames }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    private fun ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 45→        ruleProviders
 46→            .map { it.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName() }
 47→            .distinct()
 48→
 49→    private fun ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 50→        ruleProviders
 51→            .map { it.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName() }
 52→            .distinct()
 53→}
 54→
 55→/**
 56→ * Creates a filter of [RuleExecution] properties. This filter is basically an extract of the [EditorConfig] which allows to retrieve rule
 57→ * execution properties for which no proper [EditorConfigProperty] is defined as rules have no need to be able to extract such a property
 58→ * from [EditorConfig].
 59→ */
 60→private class RuleExecutionFilter(
 61→    val ruleExecutionProperties: Map<String, RuleExecution>,
 62→    val codeStyleValue: CodeStyleValue,
 63→) {
 64→    fun isEnabled(ruleProvider: RuleProvider) = isRuleEnabled(ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance())
 65→
 66→    private fun isRuleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 67→        /*
 68→         * If set for the rule, the rule execution property takes precedence above other checks. This allows for execution of a specific
 69→         * experimental or ktlint_official code style rule without enabling them all. Also, this allows to disable a specific rule in case
 70→         * the experimental and/or ktlint_official code style rules are enabled.
 71→         */
 72→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName())
 73→            ?.let { it == RuleExecution.enabled }
 74→            ?: isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule)
 75→
 76→    private fun isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 77→        when {
 78→            rule is Rule.Experimental && rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 79→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule) && isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 80→            rule is Rule.Experimental ->
 81→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule)
 82→            rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 83→                isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 84→            else ->
 85→                isRuleSetEnabled(rule)
 86→        }
 87→
 88→    private fun isExperimentalEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 89→        ruleExecution(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) == RuleExecution.enabled &&
 90→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
 91→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
 92→
 93→    private fun isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 94→        codeStyleValue == CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official &&
 95→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
 96→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
 97→
 98→    private fun isRuleSetEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 99→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName())
100→            .let { ruleSetExecution ->
101→                if (ruleSetExecution?.name == EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) {
102→                    // Rules in the experimental rule set are only run when enabled explicitly.
103→                    ruleSetExecution == RuleExecution.enabled
104→                } else {
105→                    // Rules in other rule sets are enabled by default.
106→                    ruleSetExecution != RuleExecution.disabled
107→                }
108→            }
109→
110→    private fun ruleExecution(ruleExecutionPropertyName: String) = ruleExecutionProperties[ruleExecutionPropertyName]
111→}

301→                lintError(4, 5, "$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier"),
302→            )
303→        }
304→
305→        @Test
306→        fun `Given that a NoFooIdentifierRule violation is suppressed with custom formatter tags in EOL comments then do not find a violation in that block`() {
307→            val code =
308→                """
309→                // custom-formatter-tag-off
310→                val fooNotReported = "foo"
311→                // custom-formatter-tag-on
312→                val fooReported = "foo"
313→                """.trimIndent()
314→
315→            val actual =
316→                lint(
317→                    code,
318→                    editorConfigOverride =
319→                        EditorConfigOverride.from(
320→                            FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY to true,
321→                            FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY to "custom-formatter-tag-off",
322→                            FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY to "custom-formatter-tag-on",
323→                        ),
324→                )
325→            assertThat(actual).containsExactly(
326→                lintError(4, 5, "standard:no-foo-identifier-standard"),
327→                lintError(4, 5, "$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier"),
328→            )
329→        }
330→    }
331→
332→    private class NoFooIdentifierRule(id: RuleId) : Rule(
333→        ruleId = id,
334→        about = About(),
335→    ) {
336→        override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
337→            node: ASTNode,
338→            autoCorrect: Boolean,
339→            emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
340→        ) {
341→            if (node.elementType == ElementType.IDENTIFIER && node.text.startsWith("foo")) {
342→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
343→            }
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun lint(
348→        code: String,
349→        editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
350→    ) = ArrayList<LintError>().apply {
351→        KtLintRuleEngine(
352→            ruleProviders =
353→                setOf(
354→                    // The same rule is supplied once a standard rule and once as non-standard rule. Note that the
355→                    // ruleIds are different.
356→                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
357→                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
358→                ),
359→            editorConfigOverride =
360→                editorConfigOverride
361→                    .plus(
362→                        STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
363→                        NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
364→                    ),
365→        ).lint(Code.fromSnippet(code)) { e -> add(e) }
366→    }
367→
368→    private fun lintError(
369→        line: Int,
370→        col: Int,
371→        ruleId: String,
372→    ) = LintError(line, col, RuleId(ruleId), "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
373→
374→    private companion object {
375→        val NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID = "custom".also { require(it != RuleSetId.STANDARD.value) }
376→
377→        val STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("standard:no-foo-identifier-standard")
378→        val NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier")
379→    }
380→}

21→/**
22→ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23→ */
24→public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25→    StandardRule(
26→        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28→            setOf(
29→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31→            ),
32→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33→    ),
34→    Rule.Experimental,
35→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37→        node: ASTNode,
38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40→    ) {
41→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42→            val afterBlockComment =
43→                node
44→                    .leaves()
45→                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46→                    .firstOrNull()
47→                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48→
49→            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50→                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51→                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52→            ) {
53→                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54→                if (autoCorrect) {
55→                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56→                }
57→            }
58→        }
59→    }
60→
61→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65→        rawRemove()
66→    }
67→
68→    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69→
70→    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71→    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72→        this
73→            ?.text
74→            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75→            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76→            ?.trim()
77→            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78→            ?: false
79→}
80→
81→public val NO_SINGLE_LINE_BLOCK_COMMENT_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule().ruleId
82→

1→## Why should I use ktlint?
  2→
  3→the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4→
  5→Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6→
  7→By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8→
  9→ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10→
 11→##  How do I enable or disable a rule?
 12→
 13→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 14→```editorconfig
 15→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 16→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 17→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 18→```
 19→
 20→!!! note
 21→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 22→
 23→##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?
 24→
 25→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 26→```editorconfig
 27→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 28→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 29→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 30→```
 31→
 32→!!! note
 33→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 34→
 35→## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 36→
 37→Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 38→
 39→See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 40→
 41→## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 42→
 43→!!! tip
 44→    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 45→
 46→To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 47→
 48→An error can be suppressed using:
 49→
 50→* EOL comments
 51→* Block comments
 52→* @Suppress annotations
 53→
 54→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Suppress annotation"
 55→
 56→    ```kotlin
 57→    // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
 58→    @file:Suppress("ktlint")
 59→
 60→    // Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
 61→    @Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
 62→    class Foo {}
 63→
 64→    // Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
 65→    @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
 66→    import foo.*
 67→
 68→    // Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
 69→    @Suppress("ktlint")
 70→    import foo.*
 71→    ```
 72→=== "[:material-heart:](#) EOL comments"
 73→
 74→    ```kotlin
 75→    // Suppress a single rule for the commented line
 76→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports
 77→
 78→    // Suppress multiple rules for the commented line
 79→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_other-rule-id
 80→
 81→    // Suppress all rules for the commented line
 82→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 83→    ```
 84→
 85→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Block comments"
 86→
 87→    ```kotlin
 88→    // Suppress a single rule for all code between the start and end tag
 89→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 90→    import foo.*
 91→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 92→
 93→    // Suppress multiple rules for all code between the start and end tag
 94→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 95→    import foo.*
 96→    /* ktlint-enable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 97→
 98→    // Suppress all rules for all code between the start and end tag
 99→    /* ktlint-disable */
100→    import foo.*
101→    /* ktlint-enable */
102→    ```
103→
104→!!! important
105→    When using the block comments, the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
106→
107→!!! warning
108→    From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
109→
110→## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?
111→
112→When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 
113→
114→
115→## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
116→
117→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
118→
119→A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
120→
121→Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
122→```editorconfig
123→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
124→ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
125→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
126→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
127→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
128→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
129→```
130→
131→!!! note
132→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
133→
134→!!! note
135→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
136→
137→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
138→
139→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
140→
141→## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?
142→
143→Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.
144→
145→Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.
146→
147→Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.
148→
149→!!! tip
150→    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.
151→
152→## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?
153→
154→Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
155→To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.
156→
157→!!! warning
158→    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.
159→
160→To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:
161→
162→```kotlin
163→val ruleProviders = buildSet {
164→  ServiceLoader
165→      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
166→      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
167→}
168→val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
169→  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
170→  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
171→)
172→ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
173→```
174→Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.
175→
176→It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
177→and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
178→```kotlin
179→kotlinFile.writeText(
180→  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
181→    Code.CodeSnippet(
182→      stringBuilder.toString()
183→    )
184→  )
185→)
186→```
187→
188→# Are formatter tags respected?
189→
190→As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
191→```editorconfig
192→ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
193→ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
194→ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
195→```
196→
197→When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.
198→

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
  8→
  9→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 10→
 11→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 12→
 13→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 14→
 15→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 16→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 17→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 18→
 19→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 20→
 21→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 22→
 23→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 24→
 25→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 26→
 27→### Added

 29→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))

 31→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 32→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 33→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))

 81→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 82→
 83→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 84→
 85→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 86→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 87→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 88→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 89→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 90→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 91→
 92→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 93→
 94→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 95→
 96→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 97→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 98→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 99→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
100→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
101→
102→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
103→
104→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
105→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
106→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
107→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
108→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
109→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
110→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
111→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
112→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
113→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
114→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
115→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
116→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
117→
118→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
119→
120→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
121→
122→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
123→
124→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
125→
126→#### Experimental rules
127→
128→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
129→
130→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
131→
132→#### EditorConfig
133→
134→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
135→
136→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
137→
138→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
139→
140→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
141→
142→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
143→
144→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
145→
146→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
147→
148→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
149→
150→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
151→
152→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
153→
154→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
155→

 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74→    /**
 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**
 79→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)

293→    public companion object {
294→        /**
295→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
296→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
297→         */
298→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
299→
300→        /**
301→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
302→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
303→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
304→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
305→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
306→         */
307→
308→        public fun assertThatRule(
309→            provider: () -> Rule,
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
313→
314→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
315→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
316→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
317→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
318→            { code ->
319→                KtLintAssertThat(
320→                    ruleProvider = this,
321→                    code = code,
322→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
323→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
324→                )
325→            }

327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }
353→}

355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }

417→     */

419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()

611→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
612→        val ruleProviders =
613→            setOf(ruleProvider)
614→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
615→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
616→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
617→        val editorConfigOverride =
618→            editorConfigOverride
619→                .enableExperimentalRules()
620→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
621→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
622→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
623→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
624→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
625→        )
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
629→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
630→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
631→        return lintErrors
632→    }
633→
634→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
635→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
636→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
637→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
638→    }

641→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
642→     */
643→    private data class LintViolationFields(
644→        val line: Int,
645→        val col: Int,
646→        val detail: String,
647→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
648→    )

651→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
652→    """
653→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
654→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
655→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
656→    """.trimIndent(),
657→)
658→
659→/**
660→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
661→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
662→ */
663→public data class LintViolation(
664→    val line: Int,
665→    val col: Int,
666→    val detail: String,
667→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
668→)
669→
670→/**
671→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
672→ * provided.
673→ */
674→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
675→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
676→        ruleProviders
677→            .asSequence()
678→            .map { ruleProvider ->
679→                ruleProvider
680→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
681→                    .ruleId
682→                    .ruleSetId
683→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
684→            }.distinct()
685→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
686→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
687→            .toList()
688→            .toTypedArray()
689→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
690→}
691→
692→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
693→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)

 32→/**
 33→ * Wrapping of context receiver to a separate line. Arguments of the context receiver are wrapped to separate line
 34→ * whenever the max line length is exceeded.
 35→ */
 36→public class ContextReceiverWrappingRule :
 37→    StandardRule(
 38→        id = "context-receiver-wrapping",
 39→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 40→            setOf(
 41→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 42→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 43→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 44→            ),
 45→    ),
 46→    Rule.Experimental {
 47→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 48→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 51→        indentConfig =
 52→            IndentConfig(
 53→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 54→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 55→            )
 56→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 60→        node: ASTNode,
 61→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 62→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 63→    ) {
 64→        when {
 65→            node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ->
 66→                visitContextReceiverList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 67→            node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST && node.isPartOf(CONTEXT_RECEIVER) ->
 68→                visitContextReceiverTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    private fun visitContextReceiverList(
 73→        node: ASTNode,
 74→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 75→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 76→    ) {
 77→        // Context receiver must be followed by new line or comment
 78→        node
 79→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 80→            .nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 81→            ?.takeIf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 82→            ?.let { nodeAfterContextReceiver ->
 83→                emit(nodeAfterContextReceiver.startOffset, "Expected a newline after the context receiver", true)
 84→                if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                    nodeAfterContextReceiver
 86→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
 88→                }
 89→            }
 90→
 91→        // Check line length assuming that the context receiver is indented correctly. Wrapping rule must however run before indenting.
 92→        if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
 93→            node.indent(false).length + node.textLength > maxLineLength
 94→        ) {
 95→            node
 96→                .children()
 97→                .filter { it.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER }
 98→                .forEach {
 99→                    emit(
100→                        it.startOffset,
101→                        "Newline expected before context receiver as max line length is violated",
102→                        true,
103→                    )
104→                    if (autoCorrect) {
105→                        it
106→                            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
107→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
108→                    }
109→                }
110→            node
111→                .findChildByType(RPAR)
112→                ?.let { rpar ->
113→                    emit(
114→                        rpar.startOffset,
115→                        "Newline expected before closing parenthesis as max line length is violated",
116→                        true,
117→                    )
118→                    if (autoCorrect) {
119→                        rpar.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
120→                    }
121→                }
122→        }
123→    }
124→
125→    private fun visitContextReceiverTypeArgumentList(
126→        node: ASTNode,
127→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→    ) {
130→        val contextReceiver = node.treeParent.text
131→        // Check line length assuming that the context receiver is indented correctly. Wrapping rule must however run
132→        // before indenting.
133→        if (!contextReceiver.contains('\n') &&
134→            node.indent(false).length + contextReceiver.length > maxLineLength
135→        ) {
136→            node
137→                .children()
138→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
139→                .forEach {
140→                    emit(
141→                        it.startOffset,
142→                        "Newline expected before context receiver type projection as max line length is violated",
143→                        true,
144→                    )
145→                    if (autoCorrect) {
146→                        it.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
147→                    }
148→                }
149→            node
150→                .findChildByType(GT)
151→                ?.let { gt ->
152→                    emit(
153→                        gt.startOffset,
154→                        "Newline expected before closing angle bracket as max line length is violated",
155→                        true,
156→                    )
157→                    if (autoCorrect) {
158→                        // Ideally, the closing angle bracket should be de-indented to make it consistent with
159→                        // de-intentation of closing ")", "}" and "]". This however would be inconsistent with how the
160→                        // type argument lists are formatted by IntelliJ IDEA default formatter.
161→                        gt.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
162→                    }
163→                }
164→        }
165→    }
166→}
167→
168→public val CONTEXT_RECEIVER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ContextReceiverWrappingRule().ruleId
169→
