 59→/**
 60→ * Detect and replace the deprecated `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with `@Suppress` annotations.
 61→ *
 62→ * The ktlint-disable directive with rules in the EOL comment `// ktlint-disable some-rule-id` is replaced with the
 63→ * `@Suppress` annotation which is placed on the associated declaration:
 64→ * ```
 65→ * // Before
 66→ * val foo = "foo" // ktlint-disable some-rule-id
 67→ *
 68→ * // After
 69→ * @Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule-id")
 70→ * val foo = "foo"
 71→ * ```
 72→ *
 73→ * The block `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives:
 74→ * ```
 75→ * /* ktlint-disable some-rule-id */
 76→ * val foo = "foo"
 77→ * /* ktlint-enable some-rule-id */
 78→ * ```
 79→ * are replaced with a `@Suppress` annotation as long as only a single construct (statement, declaration, expression)
 80→ * is enclosed between the directives:
 81→ * ```
 82→ * @Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule-id")
 83→ * val foo = "foo"
 84→ * ```
 85→ */
 86→@SinceKtlint("0.50", EXPERIMENTAL)
 87→public class KtlintSuppressionRule :
 88→    StandardRule(
 89→        id = "ktlint-suppression",
 90→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 91→            setOf(
 92→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 93→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 94→            ),
 95→    ),
 96→    Rule.Experimental {
 97→    private val ktlintSuppressionRuleId = RuleId("standard:$KTLINT_SUPPRESSION_RULE_ID")
 98→
 99→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
100→        node: ASTNode,
101→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
102→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103→    ) {
104→        node
105→            .takeIf { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
106→            ?.takeIf { it.isKtlintDisableDirective() }
107→            ?.let { visitEolComment(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
108→
109→        node
110→            .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
111→            ?.takeIf { it.isKtlintDisableOrEnableDirective() }
112→            ?.let { visitBlockComment(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun ASTNode.isKtlintDisableDirective() =
116→        text
117→            .removePrefix("//")
118→            .trim()
119→            .startsWith(KTLINT_DISABLE)
120→
121→    private fun ASTNode.isKtlintDisableOrEnableDirective() =
122→        text
123→            .removePrefix("/*")
124→            .removeSuffix("*/")
125→            .trim()
126→            .let { it.startsWith(KTLINT_DISABLE) || it.startsWith(KTLINT_ENABLE) }
127→
128→    private fun visitEolComment(
129→        node: ASTNode,
130→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
131→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
132→    ) {
133→        val ktlintDirectiveRuleIds = node.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds()
134→        val targetNode = node.findTargetNodeForSuppressAnnotation()
135→        if (targetNode == null) {
136→            // The EOL comment is not part of a construct that can be annotated with @Suppress
137→            emit(
138→                node.startOffset,
139→                "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in EOL comment is deprecated. The matching Suppress annotation can not be added as it is not " +
140→                    "possible to determine to which statement/declaration the annotation should be added.",
141→                false,
142→            )
143→            return
144→        }
145→
146→        val suppressionAnnotations = ktlintDirectiveRuleIds.toSuppressAnnotations()
147→        emit(
148→            node.startOffset,
149→            "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in EOL comment is deprecated. Replace with @Suppress annotation",
150→            true,
151→        )
152→        if (autoCorrect) {
153→            targetNode.addSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
154→            node.removeWithPrecedingWhitespace()
155→        }
156→    }
157→
158→    private fun visitBlockComment(
159→        node: ASTNode,
160→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
161→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
162→    ) {
163→        val directiveText =
164→            node
165→                .text
166→                .removePrefix("/*")
167→                .removeSuffix("*/")
168→                .trim()
169→        when {
170→            directiveText.startsWith(KTLINT_DISABLE) -> {
171→                visitBlockCommentDisableDirective(node, autoCorrect, emit)
172→            }
173→
174→            directiveText.startsWith(KTLINT_ENABLE) -> {
175→                // The ktlint-enable block comment will be removed when removing the ktlint-disable block comment
176→                emit(
177→                    node.startOffset,
178→                    "Directive 'ktlint-enable' in block comment is deprecated. Remove, as matching 'ktlint-disable' directive has been " +
179→                        "replaced with @Suppress annotation.",
180→                    true,
181→                )
182→                if (autoCorrect) {
183→                    node.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
184→                }
185→            }
186→        }
187→    }
188→
189→    private fun visitBlockCommentDisableDirective(
190→        node: ASTNode,
191→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
192→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
193→    ) {
194→        val ktlintDirectiveRuleIds = node.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds()
195→        val matchingEnableDirective = node.findMatchingEnableDirective(ktlintDirectiveRuleIds)
196→
197→        if (matchingEnableDirective == null) {
198→            // No matching enable directive found. The disable directive applies until end of file. In case the directive is placed at the
199→            // start of the file, it can be transformed to a file-level annotation. Otherwise, the autocorrect can not be performed.
200→            val targetNode = node.findTargetNodeForFileLevelSuppression()
201→            if (targetNode == null) {
202→                emit(
203→                    node.startOffset,
204→                    "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in block comment is deprecated. The matching Suppress annotation can not be added as it " +
205→                        "is not possible to determine to which statement/declaration the annotation should be added.",
206→                    false,
207→                )
208→                return
209→            }
210→            val suppressionAnnotations = ktlintDirectiveRuleIds.toSuppressAnnotations()
211→            emit(
212→                node.startOffset,
213→                "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in block comment is deprecated. Replace with @file:Suppress annotation",
214→                true,
215→            )
216→            if (autoCorrect) {
217→                targetNode.addFileLevelSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
218→                node.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
219→            }
220→            return
221→        }
222→
223→        val targetNode = node.findSingleCodeSiblingBefore(matchingEnableDirective)
224→        if (targetNode == null) {
225→            emit(
226→                node.startOffset,
227→                "Directive 'ktlint-disable' and 'ktlint-enable' in block comment are deprecated. The matching Suppress annotation can not " +
228→                    "be added as no single statement/declaration was found between the directives.",
229→                false,
230→            )
231→            return
232→        }
233→
234→        val suppressionAnnotations = ktlintDirectiveRuleIds.toSuppressAnnotations()
235→        emit(
236→            node.startOffset,
237→            "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in block comment is deprecated. Replace with @Suppress annotation",
238→            true,
239→        )
240→        if (autoCorrect) {
241→            targetNode.addSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
242→            node.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
243→            matchingEnableDirective.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
244→        }
245→    }
246→
247→    private fun ASTNode.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds(): List<String> =
248→        text
249→            .removePrefix("//")
250→            .removePrefix("/*")
251→            .removeSuffix("*/")
252→            .trim()
253→            .removePrefix(KTLINT_DISABLE)
254→            .removePrefix(KTLINT_ENABLE)
255→            .trim()
256→            .split("\\s+".toRegex())
257→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
258→            .map { ruleId ->
259→                // Prefix unqualified rule id with standard rule set id
260→                if (ruleId.contains(":")) {
261→                    ruleId
262→                } else {
263→                    "standard:$ruleId"
264→                }
265→            }
266→
267→    private fun List<String>.toSuppressAnnotations(): List<String> =
268→        if (isEmpty()) {
269→            // No specific rules defined, suppress all ktlint rules
270→            listOf("\"ktlint\"")
271→        } else {
272→            map { "\"ktlint:$it\"" }
273→        }
274→
275→    private fun ASTNode.findTargetNodeForSuppressAnnotation(): ASTNode? {
276→        val targetNode =
277→            // EOL comment after a leaf element should suppress the first non-whitespace parent of the leaf element
278→            prevSibling { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
279→                // EOL comment on its own line or after a comment should suppress the next sibling
280→                ?: nextCodeSibling()
281→        return when {
282→            targetNode == null -> null
283→            targetNode.psi is KtPackageDirective -> null
284→            targetNode.psi is KtImportList -> null
285→            targetNode.psi is KtImportDirective -> null
286→            targetNode.psi is KtScript -> null
287→            targetNode.psi is KtFileAnnotationList -> null
288→            else -> targetNode
289→        }
290→    }
291→
292→    private fun ASTNode.findTargetNodeForFileLevelSuppression(): ASTNode? {
293→        // Only the ktlint-disable block comment at top of the file (before the package directive) can be converted to a file level
294→        // suppression
295→        val firstCodeSibling = treeParent.children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
296→        return when {
297→            firstCodeSibling == null -> null
298→            firstCodeSibling.psi is KtPackageDirective -> {
299→                // Check if there is another code element between this node and the package directive
300→                val nextCodeSibling = nextCodeSibling()
301→                if (nextCodeSibling == null || nextCodeSibling == firstCodeSibling) {
302→                    // No code found between comment and package directive
303→                    treeParent
304→                } else {
305→                    // Code found between comment and package directive
306→                    null
307→                }
308→            }
309→            else -> null
310→        }
311→    }
312→
313→    private fun ASTNode.findMatchingEnableDirective(ktlintDirectiveRuleIds: List<String>): ASTNode? =
314→        siblings(forward = true)
315→            .firstOrNull { sibling ->
316→                sibling.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT &&
317→                    sibling.isKtlintEnableDirective() &&
318→                    sibling.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds() == ktlintDirectiveRuleIds
319→            }
320→
321→    private fun ASTNode.isKtlintEnableDirective() =
322→        text
323→            .removePrefix("/*")
324→            .removeSuffix("*/")
325→            .trim()
326→            .startsWith(KTLINT_ENABLE)
327→
328→    private fun ASTNode.findSingleCodeSiblingBefore(matchingEnableDirective: ASTNode): ASTNode? {
329→        val codeSiblings =
330→            siblings(forward = true)
331→                .takeWhile { it != matchingEnableDirective }
332→                .filter { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
333→                .toList()
334→        return codeSiblings.singleOrNull()
335→    }
336→
337→    private fun ASTNode.addSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
338→        when (val psi = psi) {
339→            is KtDeclaration -> addSuppressAnnotationToDeclaration(suppressionAnnotations)
340→
341→            is KtAnnotatedExpression -> {
342→                // Add suppression to the existing annotation entries
343→                addSuppressAnnotationToAnnotationEntry(suppressionAnnotations)
344→            }
345→
346→            is KtCallExpression,
347→            is KtLambdaExpression,
348→            -> {
349→                // Wrap the expression with @Suppress annotation
350→                wrapExpressionWithSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
351→            }
352→
353→            else -> {
354→                // Try to find a parent that can be annotated
355→                val parent = psi.parent
356→                when {
357→                    parent is KtDeclaration -> treeParent.addSuppressAnnotationToDeclaration(suppressionAnnotations)
358→                    parent is KtAnnotatedExpression ->
359→                        treeParent.addSuppressAnnotationToAnnotationEntry(
360→                            suppressionAnnotations,
361→                        )
362→                    else -> wrapExpressionWithSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
363→                }
364→            }
365→        }
366→    }
367→
368→    private fun ASTNode.addSuppressAnnotationToDeclaration(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
369→        val annotationText = "@Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})"
370→        val indent = indent(false)
371→        val modifierList = psi.getChildOfType<KtDeclarationModifierList>()
372→        if (modifierList != null) {
373→            // Check if @Suppress annotation already exists
374→            val existingSuppressEntry =
375→                modifierList
376→                    .annotationEntries
377→                    .firstOrNull { it.shortName?.asString() in listOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings") }
378→            if (existingSuppressEntry != null) {
379→                // Merge with existing @Suppress annotation
380→                existingSuppressEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
381→            } else {
382→                // Add new annotation before modifier list
383→                val modifierListNode = modifierList.node
384→                modifierListNode.addLeafElementAt(annotationText, 0)
385→                modifierListNode.addLeafElementAt("\n$indent", annotationText.length)
386→            }
387→        } else {
388→            // No modifiers exist, create a new modifier list with the annotation
389→            val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
390→            val whitespaceNode = PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n$indent")
391→            this.addChild(annotationNode, firstChildNode)
392→            this.addChild(whitespaceNode, firstChildNode.nextSibling)
393→        }
394→    }
395→
396→    private fun KtAnnotationEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
397→        val existingArguments =
398→            valueArguments
399→                .mapNotNull { it.getArgumentExpression()?.text }
400→        val newArguments = suppressionAnnotations.filter { it !in existingArguments }
401→        if (newArguments.isEmpty()) return
402→
403→        val allArguments = existingArguments + newArguments
404→        val newAnnotationText = "@Suppress(${allArguments.joinToString(", ")})"
405→
406→        val parent = node.treeParent
407→        val newAnnotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, newAnnotationText)
408→        parent.replaceChild(node, newAnnotationNode)
409→    }
410→
411→    private fun ASTNode.addLeafElementAt(
412→        text: String,
413→        position: Int,
414→    ) {
415→        val textBefore = this.text.substring(0, position)
416→        val textAfter = this.text.substring(position)
417→        (this.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(textBefore + text + textAfter)
418→    }
419→
420→    private fun ASTNode.addSuppressAnnotationToAnnotationEntry(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
421→        val annotatedExpression = psi as? KtAnnotatedExpression ?: return
422→        val existingSuppressEntry =
423→            annotatedExpression
424→                .annotationEntries
425→                .firstOrNull { it.shortName?.asString() in listOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings") }
426→        if (existingSuppressEntry != null) {
427→            existingSuppressEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
428→        } else {
429→            // Add new @Suppress annotation before the first existing annotation
430→            val annotationText = "@Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})\n${indent(false)}"
431→            val firstAnnotation = annotatedExpression.annotationEntries.first()
432→            val newAnnotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
433→            this.addChild(newAnnotationNode, firstAnnotation.node)
434→        }
435→    }
436→
437→    private fun ASTNode.wrapExpressionWithSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
438→        val annotationText = "@Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})"
439→        val indent = indent(false)
440→        val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
441→        val whitespaceNode = PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n$indent")
442→        treeParent.addChild(annotationNode, this)
443→        treeParent.addChild(whitespaceNode, this)
444→    }
445→
446→    private fun ASTNode.addFileLevelSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
447→        val annotationText = "@file:Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})\n\n"
448→
449→        // Find or create file annotation list
450→        val existingFileAnnotationList = children().firstOrNull { it.elementType == FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST }
451→        if (existingFileAnnotationList != null) {
452→            // Add to existing file annotation list or merge with existing @Suppress
453→            val existingSuppressEntry =
454→                existingFileAnnotationList
455→                    .children()
456→                    .filter { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
457→                    .mapNotNull { it.psi as? KtAnnotationEntry }
458→                    .firstOrNull { it.shortName?.asString() in listOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings") }
459→            if (existingSuppressEntry != null) {
460→                existingSuppressEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
461→            } else {
462→                // Add new annotation after existing file annotations
463→                val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
464→                existingFileAnnotationList.addChild(annotationNode, existingFileAnnotationList.lastChildNode?.nextSibling)
465→            }
466→        } else {
467→            // Create new file annotation
468→            val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
469→            this.addChild(annotationNode, firstChildNode)
470→        }
471→    }
472→
473→    private fun ASTNode.removeWithPrecedingWhitespace() {
474→        val prevWhitespace = prevLeaf()?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
475→        treeParent.removeChild(this)
476→        prevWhitespace?.let { whitespace ->
477→            if (whitespace.textContains('\n')) {
478→                // Keep the newline, just remove the content after it
479→                val text = whitespace.text
480→                val lastNewlineIndex = text.lastIndexOf('\n')
481→                val newText = text.substring(0, lastNewlineIndex + 1)
482→                if (newText.isEmpty()) {
483→                    whitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespace)
484→                } else {
485→                    (whitespace as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
486→                }
487→            } else {
488→                whitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespace)
489→            }
490→        }
491→    }
492→
493→    private fun ASTNode.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace() {
494→        val prevWhitespace = prevLeaf()?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
495→        val nextWhitespace = nextLeaf()?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
496→
497→        // Remove the comment node first
498→        treeParent.removeChild(this)
499→
500→        // Handle surrounding whitespace
501→        if (prevWhitespace != null && nextWhitespace != null) {
502→            // Merge whitespaces if both exist
503→            val prevHasNewline = prevWhitespace.textContains('\n')
504→            val nextHasNewline = nextWhitespace.textContains('\n')
505→            if (prevHasNewline && nextHasNewline) {
506→                // Keep only the newline from prev
507→                nextWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(nextWhitespace)
508→            } else if (prevHasNewline) {
509→                nextWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(nextWhitespace)
510→            } else {
511→                prevWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(prevWhitespace)
512→            }
513→        } else if (prevWhitespace != null && !prevWhitespace.textContains('\n')) {
514→            prevWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(prevWhitespace)
515→        } else if (nextWhitespace != null && !nextWhitespace.textContains('\n')) {
516→            nextWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(nextWhitespace)
517→        }
518→    }
519→
520→    private companion object {
521→        const val KTLINT_DISABLE = "ktlint-disable"
522→        const val KTLINT_ENABLE = "ktlint-enable"
523→    }
524→}

139→     * When called in [beforeFirstNode], no AST nodes will be visited. [afterLastNode] is still called.
140→     *
141→     * When called in [beforeVisitChildNodes], the child nodes of that node will not be visited. [afterVisitChildNodes]
142→     * is still called for the node and each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally
143→     * [afterLastNode] is called.
144→     *
145→     * When called in [afterVisitChildNodes] the child nodes of that node are already visited. [afterVisitChildNodes] is
146→     * still called for each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally [afterLastNode] is
147→     * called.
148→     *
149→     * Calling in [afterLastNode] has no effect as traversal of the AST has already been completed.
150→     */
151→    public fun stopTraversalOfAST() {
152→        traversalState = TraversalState.STOP
153→    }
154→
155→    private enum class TraversalState {
156→        /**
157→         * Traversal of the AST is not started. As no life cycle hooks of the [Rule] have been executed, the [Rule]
158→         * instance can not contain state specific for the AST.
159→         */
160→        NOT_STARTED,
161→
162→        /**
163→         * Traversal of the AST is started and should be continued with next node.
164→         */
165→        CONTINUE,
166→
167→        /**
168→         * Stops traversal of yet unvisited nodes in the AST. See [stopTraversalOfAST] for more details.
169→         */
170→        STOP,
171→    }
172→
173→    /**
174→     * About the rule. Background information about the rule and its maintainer. About information is meant to be used in stack traces or
175→     * API consumers to provide more detailed information about the rule. Please provide all details below, so that users of your rule set
176→     * can easily get up-to-date information about the rule.
177→     */
178→    public data class About(
179→        /**
180→         * Name of person, organisation or group maintaining the rule.
181→         */
182→        val maintainer: String = "Not specified (and not maintained by the Ktlint project)",
183→        /**
184→         * Url to the repository containing the rule.
185→         */
186→        val repositoryUrl: String = "Not specified",
187→        /**
188→         * Url to the issue tracker of the project which provides the rule.
189→         */
190→        val issueTrackerUrl: String = "Not specified",
191→    )
192→
193→    public sealed class VisitorModifier {
194→        /**
195→         * Defines that the [Rule] that declares this [VisitorModifier] will be run after the [Rule] with rule id
196→         * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId].
197→         */
198→        public data class RunAfterRule(
199→            /**
200→             * The [RuleId] of the [Rule] which should run before the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
201→             */
202→            val ruleId: RuleId,
203→            /**
204→             * The [Mode] determines whether the [Rule] that declares this [VisitorModifier] can be run in case the [Rule] with rule id
205→             * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is not loaded or enabled.
206→             */
207→            val mode: Mode,
208→        ) : VisitorModifier() {
209→            public enum class Mode {
210→                /**
211→                 * Run the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule] regardless whether the [Rule] with ruleId
212→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is loaded or disabled. However, if that other rule is loaded and enabled, it runs
213→                 * before the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
214→                 */
215→                REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
216→
217→                /**
218→                 * Run the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule] only in case the [Rule] with ruleId
219→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is loaded *and* enabled. That other rule runs before the [Rule] that declares the
220→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
221→                 */
222→                ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
223→            }
224→        }
225→
226→        public object RunAsLateAsPossible : VisitorModifier()
227→    }

229→    /**
230→     * This interface marks a rule as an 'experimental' rule. A rule marked with this interface will only be executed by ktlint in case the
231→     * '.editorconfig' allows this rule specifically or all experimental rules. This interface is used by Ktlint internally but is also
232→     * explicitly meant to be used by custom rule providers.
233→     */
234→    public interface Experimental
235→
236→    /**
237→     * This interface marks a rule as an Official rule. A rule marked with this interface will only be executed when by ktlint in case the
238→     * '.editorconfig' contains property "code_style = ktlint_official" or when enabled explicitly. This interface is intended to be used
239→     * in Ktlint internally only. It may be subject to change at any time without providing any backward compatibility.
240→     */
241→    public interface OfficialCodeStyle
242→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 4→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 5→
 6→internal class SuppressHandler(
 7→    private val suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator,
 8→    private val autoCorrect: Boolean,
 9→    private val emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
10→) {
11→    fun handle(
12→        node: ASTNode,
13→        ruleId: RuleId,
14→        function: (Boolean, (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit) -> Unit,
15→    ) {
16→        val suppress =
17→            suppressionLocator(
18→                node.startOffset,
19→                ruleId,
20→            )
21→        val autoCorrect = this.autoCorrect && !suppress
22→        val emit =
23→            if (suppress) {
24→                SUPPRESS_EMIT
25→            } else {
26→                this.emit
27→            }
28→        function(autoCorrect, emit)
29→    }
30→
31→    private companion object {
32→        // Swallow violation so that it is not emitted
33→        val SUPPRESS_EMIT: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _, _ -> }
34→    }
35→}

 38→/**
 39→ * Detect and replace the deprecated `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with `@Suppress` annotations.
 40→ *
 41→ * The ktlint-disable directive with rules in the EOL comment `// ktlint-disable some-rule-id` is replaced with the
 42→ * `@Suppress` annotation which is placed on the associated declaration:
 43→ * ```
 44→ * // Before
 45→ * val foo = "foo" // ktlint-disable some-rule-id
 46→ *
 47→ * // After
 48→ * @Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule-id")
 49→ * val foo = "foo"
 50→ * ```
 51→ *
 52→ * The block `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives:
 53→ * ```
 54→ * /* ktlint-disable some-rule-id */
 55→ * val foo = "foo"
 56→ * /* ktlint-enable some-rule-id */
 57→ * ```
 58→ * are replaced with a `@Suppress` annotation as long as only a single construct (statement, declaration, expression)
 59→ * is enclosed between the directives:
 60→ * ```
 61→ * @Suppress("ktlint:standard:some-rule-id")
 62→ * val foo = "foo"
 63→ * ```
 64→ */
 65→public class KtlintSuppressionRule :
 66→    StandardRule(
 67→        id = "ktlint-suppression",
 68→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 69→            setOf(
 70→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 71→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 72→            ),
 73→    ),
 74→    Rule.Experimental {
 75→
 76→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        node
 82→            .takeIf { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
 83→            ?.takeIf { it.isKtlintDisableDirective() }
 84→            ?.let { visitEolComment(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 85→
 86→        node
 87→            .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 88→            ?.takeIf { it.isKtlintDisableOrEnableDirective() }
 89→            ?.let { visitBlockComment(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun ASTNode.isKtlintDisableDirective() =
 93→        text
 94→            .removePrefix("//")
 95→            .trim()
 96→            .startsWith(KTLINT_DISABLE)
 97→
 98→    private fun ASTNode.isKtlintDisableOrEnableDirective() =
 99→        text
100→            .removePrefix("/*")
101→            .removeSuffix("*/")
102→            .trim()
103→            .let { it.startsWith(KTLINT_DISABLE) || it.startsWith(KTLINT_ENABLE) }
104→
105→    private fun visitEolComment(
106→        node: ASTNode,
107→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
108→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
109→    ) {
110→        val ktlintDirectiveRuleIds = node.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds()
111→        val targetNode = node.findTargetNodeForSuppressAnnotation()
112→        if (targetNode == null) {
113→            // The EOL comment is not part of a construct that can be annotated with @Suppress
114→            emit(
115→                node.startOffset,
116→                "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in EOL comment is deprecated. The matching Suppress annotation can not be added as it is not " +
117→                    "possible to determine to which statement/declaration the annotation should be added.",
118→                false,
119→            )
120→            return
121→        }
122→
123→        val suppressionAnnotations = ktlintDirectiveRuleIds.toSuppressAnnotations()
124→        emit(
125→            node.startOffset,
126→            "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in EOL comment is deprecated. Replace with @Suppress annotation",
127→            true,
128→        )
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            targetNode.addSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
131→            node.removeWithPrecedingWhitespace()
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    private fun visitBlockComment(
136→        node: ASTNode,
137→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
138→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
139→    ) {
140→        val directiveText =
141→            node
142→                .text
143→                .removePrefix("/*")
144→                .removeSuffix("*/")
145→                .trim()
146→        when {
147→            directiveText.startsWith(KTLINT_DISABLE) -> {
148→                visitBlockCommentDisableDirective(node, autoCorrect, emit)
149→            }
150→
151→            directiveText.startsWith(KTLINT_ENABLE) -> {
152→                // The ktlint-enable block comment will be removed when removing the ktlint-disable block comment
153→                emit(
154→                    node.startOffset,
155→                    "Directive 'ktlint-enable' in block comment is deprecated. Remove, as matching 'ktlint-disable' directive has been " +
156→                        "replaced with @Suppress annotation.",
157→                    true,
158→                )
159→                if (autoCorrect) {
160→                    node.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun visitBlockCommentDisableDirective(
167→        node: ASTNode,
168→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
169→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
170→    ) {
171→        val ktlintDirectiveRuleIds = node.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds()
172→        val matchingEnableDirective = node.findMatchingEnableDirective(ktlintDirectiveRuleIds)
173→
174→        if (matchingEnableDirective == null) {
175→            // No matching enable directive found. The disable directive applies until end of file. In case the directive is placed at the
176→            // start of the file, it can be transformed to a file-level annotation. Otherwise, the autocorrect can not be performed.
177→            val targetNode = node.findTargetNodeForFileLevelSuppression()
178→            if (targetNode == null) {
179→                emit(
180→                    node.startOffset,
181→                    "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in block comment is deprecated. The matching Suppress annotation can not be added as it " +
182→                        "is not possible to determine to which statement/declaration the annotation should be added.",
183→                    false,
184→                )
185→                return
186→            }
187→            val suppressionAnnotations = ktlintDirectiveRuleIds.toSuppressAnnotations()
188→            emit(
189→                node.startOffset,
190→                "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in block comment is deprecated. Replace with @file:Suppress annotation",
191→                true,
192→            )
193→            if (autoCorrect) {
194→                targetNode.addFileLevelSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
195→                node.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
196→            }
197→            return
198→        }
199→
200→        val targetNode = node.findSingleCodeSiblingBefore(matchingEnableDirective)
201→        if (targetNode == null) {
202→            emit(
203→                node.startOffset,
204→                "Directive 'ktlint-disable' and 'ktlint-enable' in block comment are deprecated. The matching Suppress annotation can not " +
205→                    "be added as no single statement/declaration was found between the directives.",
206→                false,
207→            )
208→            return
209→        }
210→
211→        val suppressionAnnotations = ktlintDirectiveRuleIds.toSuppressAnnotations()
212→        emit(
213→            node.startOffset,
214→            "Directive 'ktlint-disable' in block comment is deprecated. Replace with @Suppress annotation",
215→            true,
216→        )
217→        if (autoCorrect) {
218→            targetNode.addSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
219→            node.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
220→            matchingEnableDirective.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace()
221→        }
222→    }
223→
224→    private fun ASTNode.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds(): List<String> =
225→        text
226→            .removePrefix("//")
227→            .removePrefix("/*")
228→            .removeSuffix("*/")
229→            .trim()
230→            .removePrefix(KTLINT_DISABLE)
231→            .removePrefix(KTLINT_ENABLE)
232→            .trim()
233→            .split("\\s+".toRegex())
234→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
235→            .map { ruleId ->
236→                // Prefix unqualified rule id with standard rule set id
237→                if (ruleId.contains(":")) {
238→                    ruleId
239→                } else {
240→                    "standard:$ruleId"
241→                }
242→            }
243→
244→    private fun List<String>.toSuppressAnnotations(): List<String> =
245→        if (isEmpty()) {
246→            // No specific rules defined, suppress all ktlint rules
247→            listOf("\"ktlint\"")
248→        } else {
249→            map { "\"ktlint:$it\"" }
250→        }
251→
252→    private fun ASTNode.findTargetNodeForSuppressAnnotation(): ASTNode? {
253→        val targetNode =
254→            // EOL comment after a leaf element should suppress the first non-whitespace parent of the leaf element
255→            prevSibling { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
256→                // EOL comment on its own line or after a comment should suppress the next sibling
257→                ?: nextCodeSibling()
258→        return when {
259→            targetNode == null -> null
260→            targetNode.psi is KtPackageDirective -> null
261→            targetNode.psi is KtImportList -> null
262→            targetNode.psi is KtImportDirective -> null
263→            targetNode.psi is KtScript -> null
264→            targetNode.psi is KtFileAnnotationList -> null
265→            else -> targetNode
266→        }
267→    }
268→
269→    private fun ASTNode.findTargetNodeForFileLevelSuppression(): ASTNode? {
270→        // Only the ktlint-disable block comment at top of the file (before the package directive) can be converted to a file level
271→        // suppression
272→        val firstCodeSibling = treeParent.children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
273→        return when {
274→            firstCodeSibling == null -> null
275→            firstCodeSibling.psi is KtPackageDirective -> {
276→                // Check if there is another code element between this node and the package directive
277→                val nextCodeSibling = nextCodeSibling()
278→                if (nextCodeSibling == null || nextCodeSibling == firstCodeSibling) {
279→                    // No code found between comment and package directive
280→                    treeParent
281→                } else {
282→                    // Code found between comment and package directive
283→                    null
284→                }
285→            }
286→            else -> null
287→        }
288→    }
289→
290→    private fun ASTNode.findMatchingEnableDirective(ktlintDirectiveRuleIds: List<String>): ASTNode? =
291→        siblings(forward = true)
292→            .firstOrNull { sibling ->
293→                sibling.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT &&
294→                    sibling.isKtlintEnableDirective() &&
295→                    sibling.getKtlintDirectiveRuleIds() == ktlintDirectiveRuleIds
296→            }
297→
298→    private fun ASTNode.isKtlintEnableDirective() =
299→        text
300→            .removePrefix("/*")
301→            .removeSuffix("*/")
302→            .trim()
303→            .startsWith(KTLINT_ENABLE)
304→
305→    private fun ASTNode.findSingleCodeSiblingBefore(matchingEnableDirective: ASTNode): ASTNode? {
306→        val codeSiblings =
307→            siblings(forward = true)
308→                .takeWhile { it != matchingEnableDirective }
309→                .filter { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
310→                .toList()
311→        return codeSiblings.singleOrNull()
312→    }
313→
314→    private fun ASTNode.addSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
315→        when (val psi = psi) {
316→            is KtDeclaration -> addSuppressAnnotationToDeclaration(suppressionAnnotations)
317→
318→            is KtAnnotatedExpression -> {
319→                // Add suppression to the existing annotation entries
320→                addSuppressAnnotationToAnnotationEntry(suppressionAnnotations)
321→            }
322→
323→            is KtCallExpression,
324→            is KtLambdaExpression,
325→            -> {
326→                // Wrap the expression with @Suppress annotation
327→                wrapExpressionWithSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
328→            }
329→
330→            else -> {
331→                // Try to find a parent that can be annotated
332→                val parent = psi.parent
333→                when {
334→                    parent is KtDeclaration -> treeParent.addSuppressAnnotationToDeclaration(suppressionAnnotations)
335→                    parent is KtAnnotatedExpression ->
336→                        treeParent.addSuppressAnnotationToAnnotationEntry(
337→                            suppressionAnnotations,
338→                        )
339→                    else -> wrapExpressionWithSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
340→                }
341→            }
342→        }
343→    }
344→
345→    private fun ASTNode.addSuppressAnnotationToDeclaration(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
346→        val annotationText = "@Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})"
347→        val indent = indent(false)
348→        val modifierList = psi.getChildOfType<KtDeclarationModifierList>()
349→        if (modifierList != null) {
350→            // Check if @Suppress annotation already exists
351→            val existingSuppressEntry =
352→                modifierList
353→                    .annotationEntries
354→                    .firstOrNull { it.shortName?.asString() in listOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings") }
355→            if (existingSuppressEntry != null) {
356→                // Merge with existing @Suppress annotation
357→                existingSuppressEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
358→            } else {
359→                // Add new annotation before modifier list
360→                val modifierListNode = modifierList.node
361→                modifierListNode.addLeafElementAt(annotationText, 0)
362→                modifierListNode.addLeafElementAt("\n$indent", annotationText.length)
363→            }
364→        } else {
365→            // No modifiers exist, create a new modifier list with the annotation
366→            val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
367→            val whitespaceNode = PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n$indent")
368→            this.addChild(annotationNode, firstChildNode)
369→            this.addChild(whitespaceNode, firstChildNode.treeNext)
370→        }
371→    }
372→
373→    private fun KtAnnotationEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
374→        val existingArguments =
375→            valueArguments
376→                .mapNotNull { it.getArgumentExpression()?.text }
377→        val newArguments = suppressionAnnotations.filter { it !in existingArguments }
378→        if (newArguments.isEmpty()) return
379→
380→        val allArguments = existingArguments + newArguments
381→        val newAnnotationText = "@Suppress(${allArguments.joinToString(", ")})"
382→
383→        val parent = node.treeParent
384→        val newAnnotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, newAnnotationText)
385→        parent.replaceChild(node, newAnnotationNode)
386→    }
387→
388→    private fun ASTNode.addLeafElementAt(
389→        text: String,
390→        position: Int,
391→    ) {
392→        val textBefore = this.text.substring(0, position)
393→        val textAfter = this.text.substring(position)
394→        (this.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(textBefore + text + textAfter)
395→    }
396→
397→    private fun ASTNode.addSuppressAnnotationToAnnotationEntry(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
398→        val annotatedExpression = psi as? KtAnnotatedExpression ?: return
399→        val existingSuppressEntry =
400→            annotatedExpression
401→                .annotationEntries
402→                .firstOrNull { it.shortName?.asString() in listOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings") }
403→        if (existingSuppressEntry != null) {
404→            existingSuppressEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
405→        } else {
406→            // Add new @Suppress annotation before the first existing annotation
407→            val annotationText = "@Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})\n${indent(false)}"
408→            val firstAnnotation = annotatedExpression.annotationEntries.first()
409→            val newAnnotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
410→            this.addChild(newAnnotationNode, firstAnnotation.node)
411→        }
412→    }
413→
414→    private fun ASTNode.wrapExpressionWithSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
415→        val annotationText = "@Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})"
416→        val indent = indent(false)
417→        val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
418→        val whitespaceNode = PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n$indent")
419→        treeParent.addChild(annotationNode, this)
420→        treeParent.addChild(whitespaceNode, this)
421→    }
422→
423→    private fun ASTNode.addFileLevelSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations: List<String>) {
424→        val annotationText = "@file:Suppress(${suppressionAnnotations.joinToString(", ")})\n\n"
425→
426→        // Find or create file annotation list
427→        val existingFileAnnotationList = children().firstOrNull { it.elementType == FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST }
428→        if (existingFileAnnotationList != null) {
429→            // Add to existing file annotation list or merge with existing @Suppress
430→            val existingSuppressEntry =
431→                existingFileAnnotationList
432→                    .children()
433→                    .filter { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
434→                    .mapNotNull { it.psi as? KtAnnotationEntry }
435→                    .firstOrNull { it.shortName?.asString() in listOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings") }
436→            if (existingSuppressEntry != null) {
437→                existingSuppressEntry.mergeSuppressAnnotation(suppressionAnnotations)
438→            } else {
439→                // Add new annotation after existing file annotations
440→                val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
441→                existingFileAnnotationList.addChild(annotationNode, existingFileAnnotationList.lastChildNode?.treeNext)
442→            }
443→        } else {
444→            // Create new file annotation
445→            val annotationNode = LeafPsiElement(ANNOTATION_ENTRY, annotationText)
446→            this.addChild(annotationNode, firstChildNode)
447→        }
448→    }
449→
450→    private fun ASTNode.removeWithPrecedingWhitespace() {
451→        val prevWhitespace = prevLeaf()?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
452→        treeParent.removeChild(this)
453→        prevWhitespace?.let { whitespace ->
454→            if (whitespace.textContains('\n')) {
455→                // Keep the newline, just remove the content after it
456→                val text = whitespace.text
457→                val lastNewlineIndex = text.lastIndexOf('\n')
458→                val newText = text.substring(0, lastNewlineIndex + 1)
459→                if (newText.isEmpty()) {
460→                    whitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespace)
461→                } else {
462→                    (whitespace as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
463→                }
464→            } else {
465→                whitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespace)
466→            }
467→        }
468→    }
469→
470→    private fun ASTNode.removeWithSurroundingWhitespace() {
471→        val prevWhitespace = prevLeaf()?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
472→        val nextWhitespace = nextLeaf()?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
473→
474→        // Remove the comment node first
475→        treeParent.removeChild(this)
476→
477→        // Handle surrounding whitespace
478→        if (prevWhitespace != null && nextWhitespace != null) {
479→            // Merge whitespaces if both exist
480→            val prevHasNewline = prevWhitespace.textContains('\n')
481→            val nextHasNewline = nextWhitespace.textContains('\n')
482→            if (prevHasNewline && nextHasNewline) {
483→                // Keep only the newline from prev
484→                nextWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(nextWhitespace)
485→            } else if (prevHasNewline) {
486→                nextWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(nextWhitespace)
487→            } else {
488→                prevWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(prevWhitespace)
489→            }
490→        } else if (prevWhitespace != null && !prevWhitespace.textContains('\n')) {
491→            prevWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(prevWhitespace)
492→        } else if (nextWhitespace != null && !nextWhitespace.textContains('\n')) {
493→            nextWhitespace.treeParent?.removeChild(nextWhitespace)
494→        }
495→    }
496→
497→    private companion object {
498→        const val KTLINT_DISABLE = "ktlint-disable"
499→        const val KTLINT_ENABLE = "ktlint-enable"
500→    }
501→}
502→
503→public const val KTLINT_SUPPRESSION_RULE_ID: String = "ktlint-suppression"
504→public val KTLINT_SUPPRESSION_RULE_RULE_ID: RuleId = RuleId("standard:$KTLINT_SUPPRESSION_RULE_ID")
505→

1→## Why should I use ktlint?
  2→
  3→the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4→
  5→Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6→
  7→By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8→
  9→ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10→
 11→##  How do I enable or disable a rule?
 12→
 13→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 14→```editorconfig
 15→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 16→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 17→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 18→```
 19→
 20→!!! note
 21→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 22→
 23→##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?
 24→
 25→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 26→```editorconfig
 27→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 28→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 29→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 30→```
 31→
 32→!!! note
 33→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 34→
 35→## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 36→
 37→Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 38→
 39→See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 40→
 41→## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 42→
 43→!!! tip
 44→    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 45→
 46→To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 47→
 48→An error can be suppressed using:
 49→
 50→* EOL comments
 51→* Block comments
 52→* @Suppress annotations
 53→
 54→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Suppress annotation"
 55→
 56→    ```kotlin
 57→    // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
 58→    @file:Suppress("ktlint")
 59→
 60→    // Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
 61→    @Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
 62→    class Foo {}
 63→
 64→    // Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
 65→    @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
 66→    import foo.*
 67→
 68→    // Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
 69→    @Suppress("ktlint")
 70→    import foo.*
 71→    ```
 72→=== "[:material-heart:](#) EOL comments"
 73→
 74→    ```kotlin
 75→    // Suppress a single rule for the commented line
 76→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports
 77→
 78→    // Suppress multiple rules for the commented line
 79→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_other-rule-id
 80→
 81→    // Suppress all rules for the commented line
 82→    import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 83→    ```
 84→
 85→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Block comments"
 86→
 87→    ```kotlin
 88→    // Suppress a single rule for all code between the start and end tag
 89→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 90→    import foo.*
 91→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 92→
 93→    // Suppress multiple rules for all code between the start and end tag
 94→    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 95→    import foo.*
 96→    /* ktlint-enable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 97→
 98→    // Suppress all rules for all code between the start and end tag
 99→    /* ktlint-disable */
100→    import foo.*
101→    /* ktlint-enable */
102→    ```
103→
104→!!! important
105→    When using the block comments, the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
106→
107→!!! warning
108→    From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
109→
110→## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?
111→
112→When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 
113→
114→
115→## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
116→
117→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
118→
119→A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
120→
121→Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
122→```editorconfig
123→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
124→ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
125→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
126→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
127→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
128→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
129→```
130→
131→!!! note
132→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
133→
134→!!! note
135→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
136→
137→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
138→
139→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
140→
141→## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?
142→
143→Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.
144→
145→Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.
146→
147→Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.
148→
149→!!! tip
150→    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.
151→
152→## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?
153→
154→Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
155→To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.
156→
157→!!! warning
158→    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.
159→
160→To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:
161→
162→```kotlin
163→val ruleProviders = buildSet {
164→  ServiceLoader
165→      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
166→      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
167→}
168→val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
169→  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
170→  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
171→)
172→ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
173→```
174→Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.
175→
176→It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
177→and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
178→```kotlin
179→kotlinFile.writeText(
180→  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
181→    Code.CodeSnippet(
182→      stringBuilder.toString()
183→    )
184→  )
185→)
186→```
187→
188→# Are formatter tags respected?
189→
190→As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
191→```editorconfig
192→ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
193→ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
194→ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
195→```
196→
197→When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.

1→!!! important
  2→    Up and until Ktlint version `0.47`, experimental were located in a separate `experimental` rule set. As of Ktlint version `0.48`, each rule set can optionally define experimental rules.
  3→
  4→All experimental rules described below are part of the `standard` rule set of Ktlint. To enable all experimental rules (from all rule sets), set `editorconfig` property below:
  5→```ini
  6→[*.{kt,kts}]
  7→ktlint_experimental=enabled
  8→```
  9→Also see [enable/disable specific rules](../configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules).
 10→
 11→## Binary expression wrapping
 12→
 13→Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
 14→
 15→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 16→
 17→    ```kotlin
 18→    fun foo() {
 19→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 20→        // at the X character on the right                       X
 21→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
 22→            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
 23→                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 24→            // do something
 25→        }
 26→    }
 27→    ```
 28→
 29→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 30→
 31→    ```kotlin
 32→    fun foo() {
 33→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
 34→        // at the X character on the right                       X
 35→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
 36→            // do something
 37→        }
 38→    }
 39→    ```
 40→
 41→Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 42→
 43→## Blank line before declarations
 44→
 45→Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.
 46→
 47→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 48→
 49→    ```kotlin
 50→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
 51→
 52→    class FooBar {
 53→        val foo2 = "foo2"
 54→        val foo3 = "foo3"
 55→
 56→        fun bar1() {
 57→           val foo4 = "foo4"
 58→           val foo5 = "foo5"
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        fun bar2() = "bar"
 62→
 63→        val foo6 = "foo3"
 64→        val foo7 = "foo4"
 65→
 66→        enum class Foo {}
 67→    }
 68→    ```
 69→
 70→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 71→
 72→    ```kotlin
 73→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
 74→    class FooBar {
 75→        val foo2 = "foo2"
 76→        val foo3 = "foo3"
 77→        fun bar1() {
 78→           val foo4 = "foo4"
 79→           val foo5 = "foo5"
 80→        }
 81→        fun bar2() = "bar"
 82→        val foo6 = "foo3"
 83→        val foo7 = "foo4"
 84→        enum class Foo {}
 85→    }
 86→    ```
 87→
 88→Rule id: `blank-line-before-declaration` (`standard` rule set)
 89→
 90→!!! Note
 91→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 92→
 93→## Discouraged comment location
 94→
 95→Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 96→
 97→!!! note
 98→    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier. 
 99→
100→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
101→
102→    ```kotlin
103→    fun <T> /* some comment */ foo(t: T) = "some-result"
104→
105→    fun foo() {
106→        if (true)
107→            // some comment
108→            bar()
109→    }
110→    ```
111→
112→Rule id: `discouraged-comment-location` (`standard` rule set)
113→
114→## Disallow empty lines at start of class body
115→
116→Detect blank lines at start of a class body.
117→
118→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
119→
120→    ```kotlin
121→    class Foo {
122→        val foo = "foo"
123→    }
124→    ```
125→
126→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
127→
128→    ```kotlin
129→    class Foo {
130→
131→        val foo = "foo"
132→    }
133→    ```
134→
135→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` (`standard` rule set)
136→
137→!!! Note
138→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
139→
140→## Disallow consecutive comments
141→
142→Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
143→- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
144→- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
145→- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
146→
147→Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
148→
149→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
150→
151→    ```kotlin
152→    // An EOL comment
153→    // may be followed by another EOL comment
154→    val foo = "foo"
155→
156→    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
157→
158→    /*
159→     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
160→     */
161→
162→    /**
163→      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
164→      */
165→    fun bar() = "bar"
166→
167→    ```
168→
169→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
170→
171→    ```kotlin
172→    /*
173→     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
174→     */
175→    /*
176→     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
177→     */
178→    val bar = "bar" 
179→
180→    /**
181→      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
182→      */
183→
184→    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
185→    ```
186→
187→Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
188→
189→!!! Note
190→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
191→
192→## Function signature
193→
194→Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
195→
196→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
197→
198→    ```kotlin
199→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
200→    // at the X character on the right           X
201→    fun foooooooo(
202→        a: Any,
203→        b: Any,
204→        c: Any
205→    ): String {
206→        // body
207→    }
208→
209→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
210→    // at the X character on the right           X
211→    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
212→        // body
213→    }
214→
215→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
216→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
217→    // at the X character on the right           X
218→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
219→        .uppercase()
220→
221→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
222→    // or 'always'.
223→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
224→    // at the X character on the right           X
225→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
226→        "some-result"
227→            .uppercase()
228→    ```
229→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
230→
231→    ```kotlin
232→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
233→    // at the X character on the right           X
234→    fun foooooooo(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
235→        // body
236→    }
237→
238→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
239→    // at the X character on the right           X
240→    fun bar(
241→        a: Any,
242→        b: Any,
243→        c: Any
244→    ): String {
245→        // body
246→    }
247→
248→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
249→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
250→    // at the X character on the right           X
251→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
252→        "some-result"
253→            .uppercase()
254→
255→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
256→    // or 'always'.
257→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
258→    // at the X character on the right           X
259→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
260→        .uppercase()
261→    ```
262→
263→Rule id: `function-signature` (`standard` rule set)
264→
265→## If else bracing
266→
267→If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
268→
269→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
270→
271→    ```kotlin
272→    fun foo(value: int) {
273→        if (value > 0) {
274→            doSomething()
275→        } else if (value < 0) {
276→            doSomethingElse()
277→        } else {
278→            doSomethingElse2()
279→        }
280→    }
281→    ```
282→
283→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
284→
285→    ```kotlin
286→    fun foo(value: int) {
287→        if (value > 0)
288→            doSomething()
289→        else if (value < 0) {
290→            doSomethingElse()
291→        } else
292→            doSomethingElse2()
293→    }
294→    ```
295→
296→Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
297→
298→!!! Note
299→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
300→
301→## If else wrapping
302→
303→A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
304→
305→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
306→
307→    ```kotlin
308→    fun foobar() {
309→        if (true) foo()
310→        if (true) foo() else bar()
311→    }
312→    ```
313→
314→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
315→
316→    ```kotlin
317→    fun foobar() {
318→        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
319→        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
320→        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
321→        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
322→    }
323→    ```
324→
325→Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
326→
327→!!! Note
328→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
329→
330→## Naming
331→
332→### Function naming
333→
334→Enforce naming of function. 
335→
336→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
337→
338→    ```kotlin
339→    fun foo() {}
340→    fun fooBar() {}
341→    ```
342→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
343→
344→    ```kotlin
345→    @Test
346→    fun `Some name`() {}
347→
348→    @Test
349→    fun do_something() {}
350→    ```
351→
352→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
353→
354→    ```kotlin
355→    fun Foo() {}
356→    fun Foo_Bar() {}
357→    fun `Some name`() {}
358→    fun do_something() {}
359→    ```
360→
361→!!! note
362→    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.
363→
364→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.
365→
366→Rule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)
367→
368→### Package naming
369→
370→Enforce naming of package.
371→
372→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PackageName`.
373→
374→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
375→
376→    ```kotlin
377→    package foo
378→    package foo.foo
379→    package foo_bar
380→    package foo.foo_bar
381→    ```
382→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
383→
384→    ```kotlin
385→    package Foo
386→    package foo.Foo
387→    package `foo bar`
388→    package foo.`foo bar`
389→    ```
390→
391→Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
392→
393→### Property naming
394→
395→Enforce naming of property.
396→
397→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
398→
399→    ```kotlin
400→    val FOO_1 = Foo()
401→    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
402→
403→    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
404→
405→    class Bar {
406→        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
407→        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
408→
409→        val foo2 = "foo"
410→        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
411→
412→        // Backing property
413→        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
414→        val elementList: List<Element>
415→            get() = _elementList
416→    }
417→    ```
418→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
419→
420→    ```kotlin
421→    val Foo1 = Foo()
422→    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
423→
424→    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
425→
426→    class Bar {
427→        const val foo2 = "foo"
428→        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
429→
430→        val FOO2 = "foo"
431→        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
432→
433→        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
434→        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
435→
436→        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
437→        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
438→        val elementList2: List<Element>
439→            get() = _elementList2
440→    }
441→    ```
442→
443→!!! note
444→    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
445→
446→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
447→
448→Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
449→
450→## No empty file
451→
452→A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.
453→
454→Rule id: `no-empty-file`
455→
456→## No single line block comments
457→
458→A single line block comment should be replaced with an EOL comment when possible.
459→
460→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
461→
462→    ```kotlin
463→    /*
464→     * Some comment
465→     */
466→    val foo = "foo" // Some comment
467→    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
468→
469→    /* ktlint-disable foo-rule-id bar-rule-id */
470→    val foo = "foo"
471→    /* ktlint-enable foo-rule-id bar-rule-id */
472→    ```
473→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
474→
475→    ```kotlin
476→    /* Some comment */
477→    val foo = "foo" /* Some comment */
478→    ```
479→
480→Rule id: `no-single-line-block-comment` (`standard` rule set)
481→
482→## Spacing
483→
484→### No blank lines in list
485→
486→Disallow blank lines to be used in lists before the first element, between elements, and after the last element.
487→
488→*Super type*
489→
490→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
491→
492→    ```kotlin
493→    class FooBar:
494→        Foo,
495→        Bar {
496→        // body
497→    }
498→    ```
499→
500→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
501→
502→    ```kotlin
503→    class FooBar:
504→
505→        Foo,
506→
507→        Bar
508→
509→    {
510→        // body
511→    }
512→    ```
513→
514→*Type argument list*
515→
516→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
517→
518→    ```kotlin
519→    val foobar: FooBar<
520→        Foo,
521→        Bar,
522→        > = FooBar(Foo(), Bar())
523→    ```
524→
525→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
526→
527→    ```kotlin
528→    val foobar: FooBar<
529→
530→        Foo,
531→
532→        Bar,
533→
534→        > = FooBar(Foo(), Bar())
535→    ```

543→        val adapter1: A1,

546→        where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>, A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
547→              A2 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V2>, A2 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider {
548→        // body
549→    }
550→    ```
551→
